"I want to do something that will help all and am just laughed at. **Collecting Flower Bulbs: A Comprehensive Guide** **Step 1: Choose the Right Time to Dig Up Bulbs** The best time to dig up flower bulbs is after they have finished blooming but before the foliage dies back completely. This allows the bulb to store energy for next year's growth. For spring-blooming bulbs like tulips and daffodils, this will be in late spring or early summer; for summer-blooming bulbs such as dahlias and gladioli, you should dig them up in fall. Key Tip: Make sure the soil is dry enough to work with. If it's too wet, you risk damaging the bulbs. **Step 2: Prepare Your Tools** You'll need a few basic tools: a garden fork or spade, gloves, a bucket or container to hold the bulbs, and (optionally) a brush to clean off any excess dirt. Guideline: Sterilize your tools before use to prevent spreading diseases between plants. You can do this by wiping down the blades with rubbing alcohol. **Step 3: Gently Lift the Bulbs Out of the Ground** Insert your tool into the ground about six inches away from the base of the plant. Leverage the handle to pry up the bulb, trying not to pierce it with the fork. Once lifted, gently shake off loose soil. Important Note: Be careful not to damage the delicate roots while digging. **Step 4: Identify and Separate Bulbs** Healthy bulbs will usually divide naturally over time, creating smaller offset bulbs around the main one. These offsets can be carefully separated and planted elsewhere to produce new plants. Discard any soft, damaged, or diseased bulbs. Key Tip: Allow some of the smallest bulbs to grow another season before transplanting. They may not yet have sufficient reserves to flower properly. **Step 5: Dry the Bulbs** Spread the collected bulbs out in a single layer in a cool, well-ventilated area protected from direct sunlight. Let them air dry for a couple of days until their outer skins are papery and rustle when touched. Why? Drying prevents rot during storage. **Step 6: Clean and Store the Bulbs** Brush off remaining dirt from the dried bulbs. Label each variety clearly so you know what you've got come planting time. Store in shallow trays or net bags in a cool (but not freezing), dark place with good ventilation. Good To Know: Most bulbs prefer temperatures between 40 F - 60 F (5 C - 15 C). **Step 7: Check Regularly During Storage Period** Check stored bulbs periodically throughout the winter months for signs of mold or shriveling. Remove any affected bulbs immediately to avoid contaminating others. Remember: Proper care now means beautiful flowers later! By following these steps meticulously, you're ensuring that your flower bulbs remain healthy and ready to bloom again next year. Happy gardening! Title: How to Care for a Tooth Filling Introduction: A tooth filling is a common dental procedure used to restore teeth damaged by decay back to their normal function and shape. After getting a tooth filled, it's essential to take proper care of it to ensure its longevity and your overall oral health. This article will provide you with an in-depth guide on caring for a tooth filling, including crucial steps, explanations, tips, and guidelines. Step 1: Understand the Procedure and Healing Process After having a cavity filled, your tooth may be sensitive to temperature changes or pressure for a few days. It's important to understand that this sensitivity is temporary and should subside within one to two weeks. If symptoms persist beyond this timeframe, consult your dentist. Key Tip: Avoid chewing hard foods immediately after the procedure, as your filling needs some time to set correctly. Your dentist might recommend waiting at least 24 hours before consuming harder foods. Step 2: Maintain Good Oral Hygiene Proper oral hygiene is vital for maintaining healthy teeth and gums, especially after a filling. Brush twice daily using fluoride toothpaste, floss once per day, and rinse with an antiseptic mouthwash to remove food particles and bacteria from your mouth. These habits help prevent further decay and keep your filling clean. Guideline: Use a soft-bristled toothbrush and replace it every three months or when bristles become frayed to maintain optimal cleaning efficiency. Step 3: Watch What You Eat Stay away from sticky, hard, or crunchy foods, such as candy, nuts, and ice cubes, which can damage fillings. Opt for softer options like yogurt, cheese, and cooked vegetables instead. Also, limit sugary drinks and snacks, as they contribute to tooth decay. Key Tip: Consume fibrous fruits and vegetables, like apples and carrots, as they promote saliva production, helping wash away harmful acids and food debris. Step 4: Monitor Changes Around the Filled Tooth Keep an eye out for any signs of discomfort, pain, or sensitivity around the filled tooth. Additionally, check if there are any sharp edges or rough spots along the restoration margins. Schedule a follow-up appointment with your dentist if these issues arise. Step 5: Attend Regular Dental Checkups Regular professional cleanings and examinations are necessary for detecting potential problems early, ensuring the integrity of your filling, and preserving your overall oral health. During routine visits, dentists assess fillings for wear, leakage, or fractures and suggest appropriate action if needed. Guideline: Aim to visit your dentist every six months for a thorough examination and professional cleaning. Step 6: Address Any Pain or Discomfort If you experience persistent pain or discomfort after receiving a filling, try over-the-counter (OTC) pain relievers like ibuprofen or acetaminophen according to package directions. Desensitizing toothpaste can also alleviate sensitivity caused by exposed nerve endings. However, if home remedies don't work, contact your dentist promptly. Conclusion: Taking good care of your tooth filling involves understanding the healing process, practicing excellent oral hygiene, monitoring your restored tooth, eating sensibly, attending regular dental appointments, and addressing any pain or discomfort. By following these steps, you can extend the life of your filling and protect your smile for years to come. Truth is a noun, and the corresponding adjective is true. The word "true" also functions as a noun, a verb and an adverb. The English word "truth" is from Old English "tr ew , tr ow , tr w ", Middle English "trew e". yeah, put it on a plate please. Are you going out to dinner Wednesday night? By comparing fossils and DNA, we know that all life on Earth today had a shared ancestor, called the last universal common ancestor (LUCA). Other living things may have been alive at the same time as the LUCA, but they died out. A study from 2018 suggests that the LUCA is about 4.5 billion (4,500,000,000) years old, nearly as old as the Earth. The oldest fossil evidence of life is about 3.5 billion years old. The queen finds the safest place she can. Then she will clean it thoroughly, with her tongue, if necessary. Here she will quietly give birth. She licks the newborn kits clean. In the wild, leaving a scent is risking a dangerous encounter with other animals. The kits are born blind and with closed eyes. They "suckle" on her teats, and sleep a good deal. After two weeks or so, their eyes open. At that stage they have blue eyes, but not the best sight. A bit later, the best developed kit will totter out of the nest. The others follow. They will soon recognise you as a living thing: that is a great moment. At first, they go back to the nest to feed and sleep. After some more days they leave the nest for good, but still they may sleep together in a 'kitten heap'. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys and eat yummy food. One day, her mommy called her to come and eat dinner. "Are you ready to eat, Lily?" her mommy asked. Lily nodded and said, "Yes, mommy, I am ready!" On the plate, Lily saw some broccoli. She remembered that her mommy always said broccoli was good for her. Lily took a bite and smiled. "I like broccoli, mommy!" she said happily. Mommy was proud of Lily for trying the broccoli. They both finished their dinner and then played with Lily's toys. They had a fun time and Lily remembered to always be ready to try new things. One morning this boy Tom woke up. Part of Lesson Plan: Use of LINE in Photography Activity Overview / Details As students enter the classroom, ask them to grab their notebooks, take their seats and observe (study closely) the images on the screen in front of the classroom. After everyone has had a few minutes to look at the images before them, ask "What do these images have in common?" The most common element in these photographs is LINE. LINE is everywhere. Now, let's take a few more minutes to look around at all the LINES in our classroom . Are you wearing anything with LINES? Are the LINES we see long and straight? Are they short and thick? Maybe they're curved. What types of LINES do we see? Ask students to write down the types of lines they see in their notebook. Each student will then share one of the types of LINES they found with the rest of the class. Make a list of LINES on the board in front of the classroom. Now we are going to take a closer look at LINE and see if we can find out more information about the types of LINE we have just listed on the board. Materials / Resource - LINE Power Point [ Download ] Presents LINE in its many form with supporting photographs as visual examples. Mary had a little lamb funny little lamb a bunny oh if he had been. A record 62.8 million tourists came to New York City in 2017. Times Square is the colorful area of the Broadway Theater District, one of the world's busiest pedestrian intersections, and a famous area for the world's entertainment industry. Many of the city's landmarks, skyscrapers, and parks are known around the world. Manhattan's real estate market is one of the most expensive in the world. New York has more ethnic Chinese people outside of Asia than anywhere else in the world, with many Chinatowns across the city. The New York City Subway is the biggest single-operator rapid transit system worldwide, with rail stations. The city has over 120 colleges and universities, including Columbia University, New York University, Rockefeller University, and the City University of New York system, which is the biggest urban public university system in the United States. The world's two largest stock exchanges, the New York Stock Exchange, located on Wall Street in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan, and NASDAQ, headquartered in Midtown Manhattan, are both in Manhattan. The Iddy Biddy i recognition is an extremely important ability for emergent readers to attain. Identifying the letter i will be the primary focus of the this particular lesson. Through the activities within this lesson, students will develop the ability to recognize the letter i in print and its corresponding phoneme /i/ in spoken language. Sheila Cushman's book entitled, Tin Man Large cut-outs of the letter i (one in lower case form and one in upper case form) for a visual aide of the letters Small cut-outs of the letter i for each students to raise on their own during the Tin Man Fix-it activity A worksheet with pictures of animals with the short i sound in their names that are mixed with pictures of animals without the short i sound in their name 1. Introduce the lesson by stating the following: "There are twenty-six letters in the alphabet. Each of those letters make different sounds! It is so important that we learn what each letter looks like and what sound they make. Today, we will learn about the letter i. Listen carefully and watch my mouth as I make the sound of the letter i!" [Model how to pronounce the short i 2. "Now let's see if you can make the sound of the letter i. Let's try all together! [Practice the short vowel i sound with the students] Can you feel what your mouth is doing to make the i sound? It almost tickles at the top of your mouth doesn't it?" 3. "Now that you know how to make the letter i sound, listen to the words I say and tell me when you hear the i sound by raising your hand. Do you hear the i sound in "pig" or "pen?" [When saying the words to the student, stretch out the pronounciation of each word to emphasize the phonemes that each contain. For example, say "p-p-i-i-i-g-g" and "p-p-e-e-e-n-n."] "Now how about "tin" or "ton?" 4. "Since you did such a great job, let's see if you can see this tongue twister. I am going to say it once by myself. Listen carefully for the i sound! Say: "Sid hit Tim's hip while digging in the kit." Now I am going to write the tongue twister on the board for you to help you say it with me. [Write tongue twister on board] Now let's say it all together. "Sid hit Tim's hip while digging in the kit." 5. "Now let's say the tongue twister very slowly and stretch out the sounds in the words like this: [Model for students how to stretch out the sounds] "S-i-i-i-d h-i-i-i-t T-i-i-i-i-m's h-i-i-i-p w-h-i-l-e d-i-i-i-i-g-g-i-n-g i-i-i-n t-h-e k-i-i-i-t." Now let's stretch out the tongue twister together! [Repeat saying the stretched out version of the tongue twister with students] 6. "Now that we know the sound that the letter i makes, we are going to learn how to write the letter i on paper. [Model how to write the letter i for students during the following explanation] For the itty bitty lower case letter i, we start at the fence and go down to make a body. Don't forget to put a dot on top for the head! Let's practice writing the lower case letter i. [Repeat directions of how to write the letter and continue to model how to write it for the first few times. After a couple of tries, observe each student's ability to write the letter i on their own.] Now for the upper case letter I, we have to make a ceiling, a floor, and a wall in the middle to connect the house! Now let's try writing the upper case letter I! [Repeat the modeling and observing procedure performed during the lower case letter i instruction.] 7. Pass out copies of Sheila Cushman's book entitled, Tin Man Fix-it to the class. Read the title emphasizing the short I sounds. Before beginning to read the book, pass out paper cut-outs of the letter i. Tell students that they have to listen carefully for the i sound because everytime they hear the i sound, they must raise the letter i cut-outs 8. For a follow-up activity, distribute worksheets to the class featuring pictures of animals with the short i sound in their names that are mixed with pictures of animals without the short i sound in their name. Have students circle the animals that contain the short i sound in their name. When students have completed circling the animals, they can color their animals with crayons. Allen, Margaret. Title: How to Attire Yourself as Alex Mercer in Prototype Introduction: In this extensive tutorial, we will guide you through the process of attiring yourself as Alex Mercer, the protagonist from the popular video game series Prototype. This guide is designed to help you recreate Alex's unique look with precision and attention to detail. By following these steps, you can emulate his appearance at conventions, cosplay events, or simply for personal enjoyment. Step 1: Gathering Materials Key Tip: Research accurate references and gather high-quality materials to ensure an authentic result. To begin, collect suitable clothing items that resemble those worn by Alex Mercer. These include: * A black, form-fitting hoodie (preferably without zippers) * Black cargo pants with multiple pockets * Combat boots or sturdy, lace-up shoes * Thin, fingerless gloves * A belt with a large buckle You may also need fabric dye (black), safety pins, thread, and a sewing kit for adjustments and customization. Step 2: Preparing the Hoodie Key Guideline: Make sure your hoodie fits snugly but comfortably; modify if necessary. Examine the hoodie for fit and comfort. If required, alter its length or sleeves using a sewing machine or hand-stitching techniques. Remove any visible tags, logos, or brand names. To enhance accuracy, consider dying the hoodie black if it has a different base color. Step 3: Customizing Cargo Pants Key Tip: Choose cargo pants with numerous pockets to mimic Alex's tactical attire. Check the fit of your cargo pants, making adjustments where needed. Add additional pockets on the thighs and calves using extra fabric or purchased pocket patterns. Secure them with strong thread and reinforce with safety pins if necessary. Dye the pants black if they are another color. Step 4: Enhancing Footwear Key Guideline: Opt for durable footwear that matches Alex Mercer's combat boot aesthetic. Select appropriate footwear - either actual combat boots or lace-up shoes with similar characteristics. Ensure they are comfortable enough for extended wear. Consider adding subtle details such as shoelaces with dark hues or weathering the boots to create a more rugged appearance. Step 5: Crafting Fingerless Gloves Key Tip: Select thin material for flexibility while maintaining screen accuracy. Purchase thin, stretchy fabric in black to make fingerless gloves. Use existing glove patterns or draft one based on measurements of your hands. Cut out the pattern pieces and sew together using a sewing machine or by hand. Leave openings for fingers and thumbs. Optional: add faux leather accents along the wrist area for added detail. Step 6: Personalizing Belts & Buckles Key Guideline: Look for belts with bold buckles reminiscent of Alex Mercer's accessory. Find a belt with a substantial buckle, ideally silver or metallic in color. Alternatively, purchase separate buckles online or at craft stores and attach them to an existing belt. The design should be simple yet imposing, reflective of the character's militaristic background. Conclusion: By diligently completing each step outlined above, you will successfully attire yourself as Alex Mercer from Prototype. Remember to continually reference visual sources throughout the process to maintain accuracy. With patience, creativity, and dedication, you can convincingly portray this iconic figure in various settings and occasions. The team's colors are red and yellow. Empathy is when you see how someone else is feeling, imagine what it feels like, and then end up feeling the same way: for Smith, empathy is like "putting yourself in someone else's shoes". For example, empathy makes us feel happy to see someone else who is happy, or feel sorry for someone who is sad. Empathy may even make "us" feel pain when we see someone in pain (as if "we" had been hurt or affected). Title: How to Cancel Your FuboTV Subscription Introduction: FuboTV is a popular streaming service that offers live sports and entertainment channels. However, if you've decided that it's no longer the right fit for you, this guide will walk you through the process of canceling your subscription with ease. This tutorial aims to provide clear, concise, and practical steps to help you successfully terminate your FuboTV account. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Make sure to cancel before your next billing cycle to avoid being charged for an additional month. 2. Keep your login information handy throughout the cancellation process. 3. Double-check that your cancellation has been processed by receiving a confirmation email or checking your bank statement. 4. If you encounter any issues during the cancellation process, contact FuboTV customer support. Step 1: Log into your FuboTV Account Visit the FuboTV website (<https://www.fubo.tv/>) and click on 'Sign In' at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your FuboTV account. Logging in allows you access to your account settings, where you can manage your subscription details. Step 2: Navigate to My Account Once logged in, place your cursor over your profile picture at the top right corner of the screen. A dropdown menu will appear; select 'My Account.' This action directs you to your account management dashboard, which contains all necessary tools to modify your plan, payment methods, and more. Step 3: Access Subscription Details From the 'My Account' page, locate the 'Manage Subscription' section. Click on 'Subscription Details' to view your current plan, add-ons, and billing information. Review these details to ensure they are accurate before proceeding with the cancellation. Step 4: Initiate the Cancellation Process Underneath the 'Subscription Details,' there should be an option labeled as 'Cancel Subscription' or 'End Membership.' Click on this button to begin the cancellation procedure. Remember, do not panic when seeing this prompt - it is designed to encourage customers to reconsider their decision but won't prevent you from canceling. Step 5: Provide Feedback (Optional) Upon clicking the 'Cancel Subscription' button, FuboTV may ask you why you wish to discontinue your service. You can choose to answer honestly or skip this step entirely by selecting 'Continue to Cancel.' Answering their question might assist them in improving their services for future users. Step 6: Confirm Cancellation You will be presented with one final opportunity to change your mind regarding the cancellation. At this point, confirm your choice by pressing 'Confirm Cancellation.' Once confirmed, your membership status should update immediately, reflecting your cancellation. Step 7: Check for Confirmation Email After completing the cancellation process, look out for a confirmation email from FuboTV in your inbox. This message serves as proof that your subscription has indeed been terminated. Additionally, monitor your bank statements to verify that no further charges have occurred after canceling. Conclusion: Congratulations! By following our comprehensive tutorial, you have now successfully canceled your FuboTV subscription without any hassle. Should you decide to return to FuboTV in the future, remember that your account remains accessible using the same credentials, making resuming your service simple and convenient. The first time you see these marks, you might wonder, "What's that?" And you might worry that formattingmarks will be printed. These marks do print - they won't be on printed pages, even when you see them onthe screen.So what are formatting marks, and what do they mean? Here are a few examples:Word inserts a paragraph mark each time you press ENTER to start a new paragraph. In the picture, there'san extra paragraph mark between the two paragraphs, which means that ENTER was pressed twice. This createsextra space. Deleting the extra paragraph mark will get rid of the extra space between the paragraphs.One arrow appears each time TAB is pressed. In the picture there is one arrow in the first paragraph and twoarrows in the second paragraph, so TAB was pressed twice in the second paragraph.Dots show how many times you press the SPACEBAR between each word, or if you accidentally press theSPACEBAR between letters in a word. One dot is one space; two dots are two spaces. Normally there should beone space between each word. Dots, by the way, are different from periods at the ends of sentences. Periods(which you always see) are on the bottom of the line. Dots are higher up, toward the middle of the line.In the practice you'll see how to get rid of extra space by deleting extra formatting marks. What are those underlines in my document? As you type, Word might on occasion insert a wavy red, green, or blue underline beneath text. This indicates either a possible spelling error or that Word doesn't recognize a word, suchas a proper name or place. If you type a word that is correctly spelled, but Word doesn't recognize it, youcan add it to Word's dictionary so that it is not underlined in the future. You'll see how in the practice. Word thinks that grammar should be revised. A word is spelled correctly but does not seem to be the correct word for the sentence. For example, you type "too," but the word should be "to."What do you do about the underlines? Right-click an underlined word to see suggested revisions (every once ina while Word may not have any alternate spellings). Click a revision to replace the word in the document andget rid of the underlines. Note that if you print a document with these underlines, they will not show up on printed pages.A note of caution about green and blue underlines: Word is really good at spelling, which is prettystraightforward (most of the time). But grammar and correct word usage take some judgment. If you think thatyou are right, and Word is wrong, you can ignore the suggested revisions and get rid of the underlines. You'llsee how in the practice. If you prefer not to stop every time you see wavy underlines, you can just ignore them as you go. Whenyou are through, you can tell Word to check spelling and grammar all at one time. You'll learn how in the practice. Change page margins Basophils, or basophil granulocytes, are rare granulocytes. They appear at the sites of ectoparasite infection, or allergies. We don't know exactly how they work. Because there is in an infinite number of possible organic compounds, language is needed to give a unique name to each one. The International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry, or IUPAC, made a system for doing this. Although an IUPAC name makes every single possible molecule unique, the names are often long and complicated, so in everyday life, "trivial" names--unofficial but widely understood names--are used, such as the trivial names Paracetamol, Tylenol, and Acetaminophen, which are used for a compound whose IUPAC name is "N-(4-hydroxyphenyl) acetamide". Title: How to Check Tire Tread Depth Using a Penny Introduction: Tires are one of the most critical safety components of your vehicle. The tread depth is an essential factor in ensuring that your tires can grip the road properly, especially during inclement weather conditions. One simple method to check tire tread depth at home is by using a penny. This article will guide you through the process step-by-step, providing clear instructions and helpful tips along the way. What You'll Need: A clean and dry penny (a Lincoln head cent) Access to all four tires of your vehicle A few minutes of time Step 1: Choose a suitable location and prepare your tools. Find a flat, well-lit area where you can inspect your tires closely. Clean off any dirt or debris from the tires if necessary. Retrieve your penny and ensure it is a Lincoln head cent; other coins may have different dimensions and won't provide accurate results. Key Tip: Always examine your tires when they are cold, as driving causes tires to heat up, which might alter their pressure and give false readings. Step 2: Insert the penny into multiple grooves around the tire. Place the penny into several tread grooves across various parts of the tire, including both inside and outside edges. Make sure Abraham Lincoln's head is facing downward, with his face going into the tread. Guideline: For even more thoroughness, repeat this procedure for all four tires since wear patterns can vary among them due to factors like alignment issues or driving habits. Step 3: Observe how much of Lincoln's head is visible. If any part of Lincoln's head, specifically his hairline, remains visible above the tread, then your tire has less than 2/32 inch (1.6 mm) of remaining tread depth-the minimum legal limit in many states. If you cannot see his hairline, meaning the top of his head disappears into the tread, there is still sufficient tread left. Key Tip: Remember that just because your tires meet the minimum requirement doesn't mean they are performing optimally. Replace tires before reaching these limits for safer driving conditions. Step 4: Measure the tread depth with precision instruments (optional). While using a penny provides a general idea of tread depth, consider investing in dedicated tire gauges for greater accuracy. These devices offer measurements in fractions of an inch or millimeters. Step 5: Schedule regular inspections. Inspecting your tire tread depth every month ensures early detection of potential problems and maintains safe driving conditions. Additionally, keep track of tire rotations, alignments, and overall maintenance according to manufacturer recommendations. Conclusion: By following this comprehensive guide on checking tire tread depth using a penny, you now possess valuable knowledge about maintaining your vehicle's safety while extending its lifespan. Regularly monitoring your tire condition allows for proactive measures against hazardous situations brought upon by worn-out tires, ultimately contributing to responsible vehicle ownership. Title: How to Care for an Orphan Calf Introduction: Taking care of an orphan calf can be a rewarding experience, but it also requires dedication, knowledge, and hard work. This guide will walk you through the steps necessary to ensure the health and well-being of an orphaned calf from birth until they are weaned. It is essential to provide proper nutrition, socialization, medical care, and a safe environment for the calf to thrive. Step 1: Secure a Suitable Environment A clean, dry, draft-free shelter is crucial for the calf's comfort and health. The space should be large enough for the calf to move around freely but small enough to retain body heat. If the weather is cold, consider using additional bedding like straw or hay to keep the calf warm. Avoid using wet or soiled bedding as this may lead to illnesses. Key Tips: * Keep the shelter away from areas with high traffic or noise pollution that might stress the calf. * Regularly clean and disinfect the enclosure to prevent disease transmission. Step 2: Provide Adequate Nutrition Calves require colostrum within the first six hours after birth for optimal growth and immunity development. Fresh cow colostrum is ideal; however, if unavailable, commercial colostrum replacers can serve as alternatives. Feeding schedules depend on age: * Newborn calves (0-7 days): Feed 10% of their body weight daily divided into two feedings. Use a bottle or pail feeder. * Weaning calves (8 weeks - 4 months): Gradually transition them onto whole milk or a milk replacer. Reduce solid feed intake during this period. * Post-weaning calves (5 months+): Discontinue liquid feeding and introduce a starter ration gradually while increasing solid feed consumption. Key Guidelines: * Always use fresh, clean water when mixing formula. * Never force-feed a calf; let them set their own pace. * Monitor weight gain regularly to adjust dietary needs accordingly. Step 3: Encourage Socialization Calves are herd animals by nature, so isolation could negatively impact their behavioral development. Arrange regular interactions with other cattle or surrogate companions such as goats or dogs. However, avoid introducing new animals into the calf's environment too quickly to minimize aggression risks. Step 4: Schedule Routine Health Checks Regular veterinary visits help monitor overall health, vaccinate against common diseases, deworm, and administer any required medications. Watch out for signs of sickness including diarrhea, loss of appetite, lethargy, coughing, nasal discharge, or difficulty breathing. Consult a vet immediately if these symptoms occur. Key Points: * Maintain accurate records of healthcare interventions and growth progress. * Establish a relationship with a local large animal vet who specializes in bovines. Step 5: Teach Proper Behavior & Training Training young calves good habits early makes future management easier. Train them to drink from buckets instead of bottles, halter train, and get used to being handled gently. Patience and consistency are vital throughout this process. Remember: Raising an orphaned calf demands commitment and patience. By following these steps and seeking expert advice where needed, you can successfully rear a healthy, happy calf. People may often have a variety of eating disorders that cause them to either eat too much, or not be able to eat certain things or amounts. Common diseases like Coeliac disease or food allergies cause people to experience ill effects from consuming certain foods that are normally safe. If people eat too much food, they can become overweight or obese. This causes numerous health problems. On the other hand, eating too little food, from lack of access or anorexia could cause malnutrition. Therefore, people have to balance the amount, the nutrition, and the type of food to be healthy. Remember the flux of a vector field F through the surface S is defined by taking the double integral on the surface of F dot n dS where n is the unit normal to the surface and dS is the area element on the surface. As we have seen, for various surfaces, we have various formulas telling us what the normal vector is and what the area element becomes. For example, on spheres we typically integrate with respect to phi and theta for latitude and longitude angles. On a horizontal plane, we would just end up degrading dx, dy and so on. Every time we see z we will replace it by f of xy, whatever the formula for f might be. Let's look at yet another last way of finding n dS. And then I promise we will switch to something else because I can feel that you are getting a bit overwhelmed for all these formulas for n dS. What happens very often is we don't actually know how to parametize our surface. Maybe we don't know how to solve for z as a function of x and y, but our surface is given by some equation. And so what that means is actually maybe what we know is not really these kinds of formulas, but maybe we know a normal vector. Why would we ever know a normal vector? Well, for example, if our surface is a plane, a slanted plane given by some equation, ax by cz = d. Well, you know the normal vector. It is . Of course, you could solve for z and then go back to that case, which is why I said that one is very useful. But you can also just stay with a normal vector. Why else would you know a normal vector? Well, let's say that you know an equation that is of a form g of x, y, z equals zero. Well, then you know that the gradient of g is perpendicular to the level surface. Let me just give you two examples. If you have a plane, ax by cz = d, then the normal vector would just be . If you have a surface S given by an equation, g(x, y, z) = 0, then you can take a normal vector to be the gradient of g. We have seen that the gradient is perpendicular to the level surface. Now, of course, we don't necessarily have to follow what is going to come. Because, if we could solve for z, then we might be better off doing what we did over there. But let's say that we want to do it this. What can we do? Well, I am going to give you another way to think geometrically about n dS. Let's start by thinking about the slanted plane. Let's say that my surface is just a slanted plane. My normal vector would be maybe somewhere here. And let's say that I am going to try -- I need to get some handle on how to set up my integrals, so maybe I am going to express things in terms of x and y. I have my coordinates, and I will try to use x and y. Then I would like to relate delta S or dS to the area in the x y plane. That means I want maybe to look at the projection of this guy onto a horizontal plane. Let's squish it horizontally. Then you have here another area. The guy on the slanted plane, let's call that delta S. And let's call this guy down here delta A. And delta A would become ultimately maybe delta x, delta y or something like that. The question is how do we find the conversion rate between these two areas? I mean they are not the same. Visually, I hope it is clear to you that if my plane is actually horizontal then, of course, they are the same. But the more slanted it becomes the more delta A becomes smaller than delta S. If you buy land and it is on the side of a cliff, well, whether you look at it on a map or whether you look at it on the actual cliff, the area is going to be very different. I am not sure if that is a wise thing to do if you want to build a house there, but I bet you can get really cheap land. Anyway, delta S versus delta A depends on how slanted things are. And let's try to make that more precise by looking at the angel that our plane makes with the horizontal direction. Let's call this angle alpha, the angle that our plane makes with the horizontal direction. See, it is all coming together. The first unit about cross-products, normal vectors and so on is actually useful now. I claim that the surface element is related to the area in the plane by delta A equals delta S times the cosine of alpha. Why is that? Well, let's look at this small rectangle with one horizontal side and one slanted side. When you project this side does not change, but this side gets shortened by a factor of cosine alpha. Whatever this length was, this length here is that one times cosine alpha. That is why the area gets shrunk by cosine alpha. In one direction nothing happens. In the other direction you squish by cosine alpha. What that means is that, well, we will have to deal with this. And, of course, the one we will care about actually is delta S expressed in terms of delta A. But what are we going to do with this cosine? It is not very convenient to have a cosine left in here. Remember, the angle between two planes is the same thing as the angle between the normal vectors. If you want to see this angle alpha elsewhere, what you can do is you can just take the vertical direction. Let's take k. Then here we have our angle alpha again. In particular, cosine of alpha, I can get, well, we know how to find the angle between two vectors. If we have our normal vector N, we will do N dot k, and we will divide by length N, length k. Well, length k is one. That is one easy guy. That is how we find the angle. Now I am going to say, well, delta S is going to be one over cosine alpha delta A. And I can rewrite that as length of N divided by N dot k times delta A. Now, let's multiply that by the unit normal vector. Because what we are about is not so much dS but actually n dS. N delta S will be, I am just going to multiply by N. Well, let's think for a second. What happens if I take a unit normal N and I multiply it by the length of my other normal big N? Well, I get big N again. This is a normal vector of the same length as N, well, up to sign. The only thing I don't know is whether this guy will be going in the same direction as big N or in the opposite direction. Say that, for example, my capital N has, I don't know, length three for example. Then the normal unit vector might be this guy, in which case indeed three times little n will be big n. Or it might be this one in which case three times little n will be negative big N. But up to sign it is N. And then I will have N over N dot k delta A. And so the final formula, the one that we care about in case you don't really like my explanations of how we get there, is that N dS is plus or minus N over N dot k dx dy. That one is actually kind of useful so let's box it. Now, just in case you are wondering, of course, if you didn't want to project to x, y, you would have maybe preferred to project to say the plane of a blackboard, y, z, well, you can do the same thing. To express n dS in terms of dy dz you do the same argument. Simply, the only thing that changes, instead of using the vertical vector k, you use the normal vector i. So you would be doing N over N dot i dy dz. The same thing. So just keep an open mind that this also works with other variables. The y component, partial g, partial y is going to be negative f sub y, and g sub z is just one. What it says is if S is a closed surface -- Remember, it is the same as with Green's theorem, we need to have something that is completely enclosed. You have a surface and there is somehow no gaps in it. There is no boundary to it. It is really completely enclosing a region in space that I will call D. And I need to choose my orientation. Title: How to Boil an Egg Perfectly Boiling an egg may seem like a simple task, but achieving the perfect boiled egg with a creamy yolk and tender white can be quite a challenge. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to boil an egg to perfection, including key tips and guidelines to ensure clarity and practicality. **Things You'll Need:** * Fresh eggs * Saucepan or pot * Cold water * Slotted spoon * Timer * Bowl of ice water **Step 1: Choose Your Eggs** Fresh eggs are more difficult to peel than older eggs, so if possible, use eggs that are a few days old. If you only have fresh eggs, adding baking soda to the water can help make them easier to peel. **Step 2: Place the Eggs in the Pot** Gently place your eggs in a single layer at the bottom of a saucepan or pot. Avoid stacking the eggs as this could lead to uneven cooking or cracking. **Step 3: Fill the Pot with Water** Add enough cold water to the pot to cover the eggs by about one inch (2.5 cm). The amount of water needed may vary depending on the size of your pot and the number of eggs you're boiling. **Step 4: Slowly Bring the Water to a Boil** Place the pot on the stove over medium heat and bring the water to a gentle boil. A rolling boil is too hot and could cause the eggs to crack. It usually takes around 8-10 minutes for the water to reach a boil. **Key Tip:** Adding a pinch of salt to the water can help prevent the eggs from cracking while they cook. **Step 5: Turn Off the Heat and Cover the Pot** Once the water reaches a boil, turn off the heat immediately. Cover the pot with a lid. **Step 6: Set a Timer** Depending on how well done you want your yolks, set a timer for the following durations: * Soft-boiled: 4-5 minutes * Medium-boiled: 9-12 minutes * Hard-boiled: 12-15 minutes Remember, these times are approximate and will depend on various factors such as the size of the eggs, starting temperature, and altitude. Adjust accordingly based on personal preference. **Step 7: Transfer the Cooked Eggs to Ice Water** After the timer goes off, carefully drain the hot water from the pot using a slotted spoon. Immediately transfer the cooked eggs into a bowl filled with ice water. Let them cool completely before peeling, which should take approximately 10-15 minutes. Cooling the eggs quickly stops the cooking process, preventing the yolks from turning gray and making them easier to handle and peel. **Peeling the Egg** 1. Gently tap the egg on a hard surface until the shell is finely crackled all over. 2. Roll the egg between your hands to loosen the shell. 3. Starting peeling at the large end, keeping the egg under cold running water helps to remove the shell. 4. Pat the peeled egg dry with a clean towel or paper towels. Congratulations! You now know how to perfectly boil an egg. Enjoy your delicious and nutritious snack! Although birds are warm-blooded creatures like mammals, they do not give birth to live young. They lay eggs as reptiles do, but the shell of a bird's egg is hard. The baby bird grows inside the egg, and after a few weeks hatches (breaks out of the egg). 'Transgender' is a term that means that the gender assigned at birth (typically 'AMAB' or 'assigned male at birth' and 'AFAB' or 'assigned female at birth') does not match with the gender a person identifies as. The experiences that transgender people have are vast and may differ from one another, although there are sometimes shared experiences. For instance, a transgender person may feel gender dysphoria (but not everyone will be dysphoric). A transgender person may also feel it necessary to medically transition, or take hormones, or a combination of the two. 'Transsexual' is a term that some transgender people may use after receiving sex-reassignment surgeries and medical transition, but depending on who one is talking to, this term may be considered outdated and the person may instead opt to simply continue using the term 'transgender' as their identification. He had been so careless, he couldn t keep his footing. He had been running too fast and too far, with too much weight in his hands. [removing puppet from bag while Mac is playing with a car] Title: How to Buy Cheap Books: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Books are an essential part of personal growth and development, but purchasing them can sometimes be expensive. This guide will walk you through various strategies and platforms that allow you to buy cheap books without compromising quality or selection. By following these steps, you'll learn how to build your library while saving money. Step 1: Utilize Online Marketplaces Online marketplaces like Amazon, eBay, and Alibris offer competitive prices on new and used books. These websites have extensive catalogues, making it easy to find what you need at discounted rates. Key Tips: - Check multiple sellers for the same book to compare prices. - Read customer reviews before buying from unfamiliar sellers. - Look out for promotions and deals offered by the platform. Step 2: Explore Library Sales and Book Swaps Libraries often host sales where they sell donated or withdrawn items at low costs. Additionally, community centers may organize book swaps where participants exchange their pre-owned books. Guidelines: - Research local libraries and community centers for upcoming events. - Bring cash as some venues might not accept card payments. - Consider donating unwanted books to support future sales and swaps. Step 3: Visit Thrift Stores and Charity Shops Thrift stores and charity shops frequently carry second-hand books at affordable prices. These establishments usually price their inventory lower than dedicated bookstores due to high turnover rates. Key Points: - Regularly visit different locations as stock changes frequently. - Don't shy away from damaged covers; check if the pages inside are still intact. - Supporting non-profit organizations is an added benefit when shopping at charity shops. Step 4: Use Price Comparison Websites Websites such as BookFinder, AddALL, and Biblio allow users to search for specific titles across numerous online retailers and physical stores. They compile results based on availability and pricing so you can make informed decisions. Important Notes: - Create accounts with these services to receive alerts about price drops or restocks. - Compare shipping fees alongside base prices to ensure overall savings. - Be aware of potential delays in delivery times from certain vendors. Step 5: Join Book Clubs and Subscription Services Joining a book club or signing up for a subscription service provides access to exclusive discounts and special offers. Many clubs also share resources and recommendations among members. Suggestions: - Browse social media groups, meetup apps, and literary magazines for local book clubs. - Investigate monthly subscription boxes tailored to specific genres or age ranges (e.g., BookCase.Club). - Negotiate bulk purchases with suppliers if starting your own book club. Step 6: Leverage Digital Platforms Digital formats like ebooks and audiobooks tend to cost less than print editions. Platforms like Kindle, Google Play Books, Kobo, and Audible provide vast selections at reduced rates compared to traditional publishers. Helpful Hints: - Download free samples before committing to full purchase. - Take advantage of limited-time promotional prices. - Consider sharing subscriptions with friends or family members to split costs. Conclusion: Building a robust collection of books doesn't require breaking the bank. With patience, resourcefulness, and knowledge of available options, anyone can buy cheap books while supporting authors, publishers, and local communities. Happy reading! The end of this day marks the end of the first third of a leap year. It also falls on the same day of the week as New Year's Day of the following year and additionally, New Year's Eve of the following year in years immediately before a common year. The end of the first third of the year for southern hemisphere DST countries occurs at 11:00 p.m. The passive voice is used in English when the subject is unknown or unimportant. We form it by using the verb to be (in the appropriate tense) plus the past participle. For example. The president was interviewed. (We don't know who interviewed the president.) The child will be punished. (We don't know who will punish the child.) The door has been closed. (We don't know who or what closed the door.) Sometimes, we do know but don't care, or it's not important. For example: The president was interviewed by the journalist The child will be punished by his parents. The door was closed by the guard. When we do care, or when the subject (the doer) is important we use the active voice: The journalist interviewed the president. The child's parents will punish him. The guard opened the door. - For Teachers Title: How to Deliver Eye Medicine to Rabbits Introduction: Rabbits are prone to various eye conditions such as conjunctivitis or pink eye, which can be painful and contagious if left untreated. Administering eye medication to rabbits may seem challenging due to their sensitive nature, but with patience and proper techniques, you can ensure your pet receives the necessary treatment. This article will provide an in-depth guide on delivering eye medicine to rabbits while highlighting essential tips and guidelines for achieving optimal results. Step 1: Gather Necessary Supplies Before starting, gather all required supplies, including: * The prescribed eye medication (ointment or drops) from your veterinarian * Cotton balls or tissues * Clean water * A towel or blanket (optional) Having everything ready will minimize stress for both you and your rabbit during the process. Step 2: Create a Calm Environment Choose a quiet space free from distractions where you can comfortably handle your rabbit. Place a towel or blanket over your lap to create a soft surface and prevent slipping. Dimming lights might help relax your rabbit further. Step 3: Approach Your Rabbit Carefully Approach your rabbit slowly and calmly to avoid startling them. Speak softly and offer gentle strokes before picking up your rabbit. Be mindful that sudden movements could cause fear and resistance. Step 4: Securely Hold Your Rabbit Place one hand under their chest, supporting their body weight while using your other arm to gently hold their hindquarters against your body. This position is known as "burrito wrapping" and allows you to maintain control without causing discomfort or injury. Key Tip: Never force open your rabbit's eyes, as this could damage their delicate eyelids or tear ducts. Instead, let them adjust to being held before proceeding with administering the medicine. Step 5: Apply Eye Medicine For ointments, tilt your rabbit's head back slightly and pull down the lower lid gently. Position the tube above the lower eyelid and squeeze out a small amount (as directed by your vet). Allow your rabbit to blink naturally, spreading the medication across the eyeball's surface. For eye drops, follow similar steps but hold the dropper directly above the eye without touching it. Squeeze one drop into the inner corner of the eye, letting gravity carry the liquid onto the eyeball. Avoid blinking or moving quickly after applying the drops, as this may encourage your rabbit to rub its eyes excessively. Step 6: Reward and Monitor After successfully administering the eye medicine, reward your rabbit with treats and praise. Keep monitoring their behavior and appearance for any adverse reactions, like swelling or increased redness. If these symptoms occur, contact your veterinarian immediately. Guidelines for Successful Administration: 1. Always wash your hands before handling the medication and your rabbit to prevent cross-contamination and infection. 2. Store the eye medication according to label directions to preserve potency and effectiveness. 3. Regularly clean any residual medication off your rabbit's fur to prevent irritation. 4. Consult your veterinarian if you encounter difficulties or have concerns about properly administering the eye medication. Conclusion: Administering eye medication to rabbits requires patience, care, and understanding. By following this comprehensive guide and implementing our suggested tips, you can confidently deliver the necessary treatment to keep your beloved pet healthy and comfortable. A reed is a piece of dry bamboo that is used in some musical instruments such as saxophones, clarinets and oboes. The musician blows air through the mouthpiece where the reed is firmly placed, and the air makes the reed vibrate; this vibration in the mouthpiece produces sound all along the instrument, which is changed into specific musical notes depending on the physical nature of the instrument. Title: How to Date a Police Officer Dating a police officer can be both exciting and challenging due to their unique work schedule and demanding job responsibilities. However, with patience, understanding, and effective communication, you can build a fulfilling relationship with someone who serves and protects your community. Here's an extensive guide on how to date a cop while addressing potential obstacles and highlighting essential aspects of such a partnership. ### Step 1: Understand Their Work Schedule Police officers often have unpredictable schedules that include working nights, weekends, and holidays. Before pursuing a relationship, ensure you understand this reality and are comfortable adapting to accommodate their shifts. This may involve adjustments to your social life or personal time management but demonstrates empathy towards their commitment. Key Tip: Establish open communication early regarding availability and expectations around spending quality time together. ### Step 2: Show Genuine Interest in Their Job Ask questions about their experiences at work without prying into confidential matters. Express curiosity about what drew them to law enforcement and learn more about its various facets. By showing genuine interest, you validate their career choice and create opportunities for deeper connections based on shared respect and admiration. Guideline: Avoid discussing controversial topics related to policing until trust has been established between you two. ### Step 3: Be Patient With Emotional Availability Officers might carry emotional burdens from their line of duty, making it difficult for them to fully engage during off-hours. Practice patience when they seem preoccupied or distant, offering support as needed rather than pressing for immediate attention. Over time, consistency in expressing care will foster stronger bonds. Key Tip: Encourage self-care activities like exercise, hobbies, and therapy to help manage stress levels and maintain overall wellbeing. ### Step 4: Develop Strong Communication Skills Effective communication is crucial when dating a cop since emergencies can arise unexpectedly, requiring quick decisions and adaptability. Honesty, transparency, and active listening should form the foundation of any conversation. Being able to discuss challenges openly will strengthen your bond and equip you better for navigating through tough times. Guideline: Regularly check in with one another about feelings and needs, ensuring neither partner feels neglected or ignored. ### Step 5: Cultivate Independence See if she'll look at the XXX. The word acrostic if formed from the Greek word acros (outermost) and stichos (line of poetry). You can even find acrostic in the Bible in a book called the Psalms. The acrostic is a poem where the first letter of each line form a word when you read them looking downward. A very complicated acrostic would have the last letter of each line forming a separate word as you read downward, also. But let's take one step at a time and concentrate on simple acrostics. Follow the directions for completing your own. Lyrical Lesson: Acrostic 1. Write the first name of someone you admire. 2. Think about how to describe that person and what they mean to you. 3. On scratch paper, write out your ideas. Proofread. Revise. 4. Using a word processing program, type the letters that will form your acrostic (The person's name.). Have a teacher show you how to change the color of the font. Make your first letters a different color than the rest of your poem. Also capitalize them and use bold function on the tool bar of your processing program. 5. Use your cursor to align the rest of your poem. 6. Proof read. Use spell check. Revise. 7. The eardrum is a thin flap of skin that is stretched tight like a drum and vibrates when sound hits it. These vibrations move the tiny bones of the middle ear, which send vibrations to the inner ear. There were some large-scale battles during the Vietnam War however most of the fighting was guerilla warfare. This type of warfare is different from the large-scale battles fought between armies, like those in World War II.In guerilla warfare, small units fight limited battles against an enemy force, set up ambushes, make surprise attacks, and then retreat back into the countryside or blend into the local population. Title: How to Date on World of Warcraft (WoW) Introduction: World of Warcraft (WoW) is one of the most popular massively multiplayer online role-playing games (MMORPGs) with millions of active players worldwide. With such a vast community, it's no surprise that many players are looking for love or companionship within the game. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about dating on WoW. Step 1: Create an Authentic Character Key Tip: Be Genuine When creating your character, be true to yourself. Choose a race and class that resonates with who you are in real life. Honesty attracts authentic people; therefore, starting off as genuine sets the foundation for potential relationships. Guideline: Your character creation says a lot about you before even saying hello. Make sure it reflects positively on you. Step 2: Level Up Your Social Skills Key Tip: Engage in Conversations Interacting with other players is essential when trying to date on WoW. Start by engaging in casual conversations during quests, dungeons, raids, or simply hanging out at city hubs like Orgrimmar or Stormwind. Guideline: Practice makes perfect. The more you communicate, the better your social skills become, increasing your chances of finding a compatible partner. Step 3: Find Like-Minded Players Key Tip: Join Communities/Guilds Join communities or guilds related to your interests outside gaming, e.g., book clubs, fitness groups, music lovers, etc. You're likely to meet individuals sharing similar values and hobbies, making meaningful connections easier. Guideline: Participate regularly in guild activities and contribute constructively. People appreciate those who add value to their group. Step 4: Identify Potential Partners Key Tip: Look Beyond Appearance In a virtual world where avatars can look however we want them to, focus on getting to know someone based on their personality, humor, intelligence, and shared passions rather than physical appearance. Guideline: Spend time chatting with different people, observing their behavior, and listening to their stories. Over time, you'll naturally identify those whom you connect with deeply. Step 5: Establish Contact Outside the Game Could also just be things he's able to pick up. And the bullets didn't go in my nose. Following the success of the 1896 Games, the Olympics entered a period of stagnation that threatened their survival. The Olympic Games held at the Paris Exposition in 1900 and the World's Fair at St. Louis in 1904 were side-shows. The Games at Paris did not have a stadium, however this was the first time women took part in the games. The St. Louis Games hosted 650 athletes, but 580 were from the United States. The homogeneous nature of these celebrations was a low point for the Olympic Movement. The Games rebounded when the Intercalated Games (so-called because they were the second Games held within an Olympiad, a period of time lasting four years) were held in Athens. These Games are not officially recognized by the IOC and no Intercalated Games have been held since. These Games were hosted at the Panathenaic stadium in Athens. The games attracted an international field of participants and generated great public interest. " He makes lightning splat splat splat. " Nobody's going to try and mug me in the snow. Before World War II, doctors and hospitals were getting better at collecting (or getting and keeping) data about their patients who had cancer, but it was rare for this data to be shared with other doctors or hospitals. This changed after WWII, when medical research centers found out that different countries had very different number of cases of cancer. Because of this, many countries created national public health organizations (which studied public health issues in an entire country). These national public health organizations began to bring together health data from many different doctors and hospitals. This helped them figure out some of the reasons why cancer was so much more common in certain places. For example, in Japan, people studying cancer found out that people who had survived the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki had bone marrow that was completely destroyed. This helped them realize that diseased bone marrow could also be destroyed with radiation, which was a very important step in figuring out that leukemia (a blood cancer) can be treated with bone marrow transplants. Mars does not have a global magnetic field. Despite this, observations show that parts of the planet's crust have been magnetized. This suggests that polarity reversals have occurred in the past. This paleomagnetism is similar to the magnetic striping found on Earth's ocean floors. One theory is that these bands suggest plate tectonic activity on Mars four billion years ago, before the planetary dynamo stopped working and the planet's magnetic field faded. And he puts a piece of paper in her present. Hello young mathematicians! Today, we're going to learn about something called "separable differential equations." Don't worry if the name sounds complicated - by the end of this, you'll understand it easily! Firstly, remember when we learned about functions? We said that a function takes an input (x), does something with it, and then gives us an output (y). In other words, y = f(x). Well, today we're looking at special kinds of functions where both x and y change together based on some rule - these are called "differential equations". They show how fast or slow y changes depending on the value of x. Now imagine you have a box with two balls bouncing around inside. You want to find out how their speeds change over time. Let's call one ball A and the other B. Their speed rules could be written as dA/dt = 3*A and dB/dt = 4*B . These tell us that A's speed increases three times faster than its current speed each second, while B's speed doubles every second. The 'd' here stands for "change", so dA/dt really means "the change in A with respect to t" (time). Our goal is to separate variables - meaning isolating As and Bs on opposite sides of the equal sign - making it easier to solve. So for ball A, we get dt/(1/A)=3dt and similarly for ball B, dt/(1/B)=4dt. By integrating both sides, we find that ln|A|=3t+C and ln|B|=4t+D , which give us exponential growth formulae for the velocities of the balls! And voila, we solved our first set of separable differential equations! Remember, real life has lots of situations similar to our bouncy balls example; think of a car accelerating down the road, plants growing towards sunlight, or even your savings account growing with interest! All those scenarios involve changing quantities related through rates of change, just like separable differential equations. Happy problem solving! Following the takeover of the Popeye animated franchise by Paramount Studios in 1942, the Popeye cartoons were now handled by Famous Studios, which made drastic changes which abandoned almost all traces of "Thimble Theatre" and focused largely on plots involving Popeye, Olive, Bluto in something resembling a love triangle, without many other characters appearing and with very few shorts deviating from that setup, which involved Olive falling for Bluto and Popeye beating him after eating spinach in an oft-repeated formula. Also of note are Famous Studios' remakes of Fleischer cartoons (such as "Goonland", itself a loose "Thimble Theatre" adaptation), heavily changed by Famous to remove all traces of Segar creations outside of Popeye, Bluto and Olive, i.e. "Popeye's Pappy" replacing "Goonland"'s Goons with what might be considered racial caricatures of Africans led by a Bluto-like cannibal, with the only thing in common between the two versions being the rescue of Pappy - who would go on to make only two more appearances after this (one of which contradicted the established fact that he left his son shortly after his birth). Supporting characters who were notable friends of Popeye and even had several appearances in Fleischer cartoons, such as Wimpy, Pappy and Swee'Pea, were featured less than a handful of times: Wimpy only appeared in three shorts (aside from flashback-oriented stories) and others such as Eugene the Jeep never appeared at all. Popeye's role as an adventuring sailor was also reduced to a point where he was barely ever seen at sea or in the Navy other than in a few shorts mostly from the war period, as the majority of stories preferred to avoid doing more adventurous and fantastical stories and focus on Popeye's everyday life, his vacation hijinks or the "love triangle" setup. [First home visit; took place in living room; CHI sat on floor and MOT joined her and sat alternately at her side and across from her] You've done a great job with this. Alright Chi go sit down this is real hot. Title: How to Hold a Butterfly Butterflies are delicate and beautiful creatures that can be fascinating to observe up close. However, it's important to handle them carefully if you need to move or examine them. This tutorial will guide you through the process of holding a butterfly safely and humanely. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** 1. Always wash your hands before handling any insect. The oils from your skin can harm the butterfly's scales. 2. Avoid touching the wings directly whenever possible. Touching the wings could damage their fragile scales. 3. Limit the time spent handling the butterfly as much as possible. Prolonged exposure to air and heat from our bodies can dehydrate and potentially kill these sensitive creatures. 4. Never pick up a butterfly by its wings. 5. Have patience; butterflies may fly away when startled so approach slowly and calmly. **Step 1: Prepare Your Tools** You won't need many tools but having some soft-tip tweezers or a small paintbrush nearby can make things easier. These can help gently lift the butterfly without causing harm. **Step 2: Approach Slowly** Approach the butterfly slowly and calmly. Quick movements might scare it off. If there are flowers around, try positioning yourself near one - the butterfly may return to it after being disturbed. **Step 3: Cup Your Hands** Cup both hands together creating a little enclosed space. Make sure your fingers are slightly apart leaving enough room for the butterfly to enter. **Step 4: Guide the Butterfly Gently** Use your chosen tool (soft-tip tweezers or brush) to gently nudge the underside of the butterfly into your cupped hands. Be careful not to squeeze too hard or touch its wings with your tools. **Step 5: Close Your Hands Carefully** Once the butterfly has entered your makeshift 'cage', bring your other hand over the top gently. Try to match the shape of your bottom hand so as not to squash the butterfly. **Step 6: Secure the Butterfly** Gently press the edges of your hands together, trapping air inside. The butterfly should now be secure but still able to breathe. Remember though, this is only temporary! **Step 7: Observe Mindfully** Take a moment to appreciate the beauty of the creature in your hands. Look at its colors, patterns, and body structure. But remember, do not hold onto it for longer than necessary. **Step 8: Release the Butterfly** To release the butterfly, place your open hands gently down where you found it or near a flower. It should naturally open its wings and fly away once it feels safe. Remember, observing wildlife doesn't always require handling. Use discretion and consider whether holding the butterfly is truly necessary. Enjoy nature respectfully and responsibly! [trochee, with a syllabic nasal; 27 sec. THE FOX, though in general more inclined to roguery than wit, had once a strong inclination to play the wag with his neighbour the Stork. He accordingly invited her to dinner in due form. But when she came to the table, the Stork found it consisted entirely of different soups, served in broad, shallow dishes, so that she could only dip the end of her bill in them, but could not possibly satisfy her hunger. The Fox lapped them up very readily, and every now and then addressing himself to his guest, desired to know how she liked her entertainment, hoped that everything was to her liking, and protested he was very sorry to see her eat so sparingly. The Stork, perceiving she was jested with, took no notice, but pretended to like every dish extremely; and, at parting, pressed the Fox so earnestly to return her visit that he could not, in civility, refuse. [so fried it's hard to make out; 1.3 sec] Once upon a time there was a giant tree in a hidden forest. Every night an owl would come and rest in its branches. One night the owl heard a strange noise. The owl looked down and noticed a body in the clearing. Curious, the owl flew down and investigate. The owl asked, Who are you? The body replied, I am the guardian of the hidden forest. New York City started as a trading post created by colonists from the Dutch Republic in 1624 on Lower Manhattan; the post was named New Amsterdam in 1626. In 1664, the English controlled the city and the areas around it, and were renamed "New York" after King Charles II of England gave the lands to his brother, the Duke of York. New York was the capital of the United States from 1785 until 1790, and has been the biggest U.S. city since 1790. The Statue of Liberty welcomed millions of immigrants as they came to the U.S. by ship in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, and it is a symbol of the U.S. and its ideals of liberty and peace. In the 21st century, New York has grew into a global hub of creativity and entrepreneurship and environmental sustainability, and as a symbol of freedom and cultural diversity. In 2019, New York was voted the best city in the world in a survey of over 30,000 people from 48 cities worldwide, because of its cultural diversity. Changes in the higher qualities of culture (sometimes called the "Superstructure") are often linked to changes in the lower qualities of life (sometimes called the "Base"). One example is that in medieval times, people thought "honor" or duty to people with more power than them was very important. Today, in Western countries, many people see ambition (being someone who works hard for their own goals) as more important. This is because in medieval times, people worked their entire lives under lords who depended on them not only for work but for war. Today, people work for themselves more, and our society lets some people move up from being poor to being rich. In this case, what people see as good and important depends on how the rulers get value out of their workers. I think all of them she did. You have lots of paper in there. Well that will be a real noisy sound. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/HSLLD/HV1/ER/remer1.cha = = = Guest Author - Marie Rippel Learning to read is like a progressive climb; your students will base what they learn off of what they have already learned, and in vowel team syllables, this is certainly the case. If your students are comfortable with the first three syllable structures (closed syllables, open syllables, and vowel-consonant-e syllables), it is time to introduce them to vowel team syllables. In vowel team syllables, there are two vowels that are side by side. Together, they can create a new sound. An example of this would be the word bread, where the e and the a work together to produce the short e sound. Other vowel team examples include oi, oy, ey, oa, oo, ou, ui, and ai. Examples of one syllable words which contain vowel teams: boil, coat, fruit, head, out and rain. Multisyllabic words that use vowel teams include author, cartoon, around, explain, remain, payment and ideal. Don't rely on the old adage, "when two vowels go walking, the first one does the talking." This is an unreliable tool to teach kids as it is not always true; for example, look the words head and bread. In fact, this adage is wrong more often than it is correct! Make sure that your students are aware that while word like goat or bead take on the first vowel's sound, words like sauce and book do not. As an alternative to the unreliable "when two vowels go walking" rule, introduce them to phonograms. Phonograms are letters and letter combinations that make one sound. (Sh is a phonogram, and says the sound /sh/ at the beginning of words. Oi is a phonogram which says /oy/ in the middle of words. Some of the phonograms have several different sounds, depending upon the word in which it is found. Take the vowel team ea; it can say /E/, /e/ or /A/. The first sound, long e, is the most common sound of ea, while the last sound, long a, is the least common. When he comes across an unfamiliar word, the student who knows the sounds of the phonograms can be taught to try the first sound, first. In the word leap, for instance, the reader who doesn't already know the word can test out the /E/ sound. This is the correct choice. In the word bread, the beginning reader tests out the second sound, and finds that he can sound out this word. Knowledge of the phonograms is a great way to help learners narrow down the choices that they have when trying to decipher a certain word. With this tool under your students' belts, they'll be further along on the road to reading independently. Understanding vowel team syllables is an integral part to understanding the written language. Usually, the programming language uses real words for some of the commands (e.g. "if... then... else...", "and", "or"), so that the language is easier for a human to understand. Like any normal language, many programming languages use punctuation. Many programs are then compiled, which means that the computer changes, or translates, the source code into another language (such as assembly language or machine language) that a computer can read, but which is much harder for a person to read. they're going to have a birthday cake for you. We will commonly see lines expressed standard form, especially when we look at and write systems of linear equations. The standard form of a line puts the x and y terms on the left hand side of the equation, and makes the coefficient of the x-term positive. While standard form is commonly, we sometimes rewrite a line in slope-intercept form in order to graph it. So standard form of a line is probably the least practical form of a line but it is something that we see from time to time so it is good to understand how it is used. So what standard form is, is an equation of the line ax+by=c. And what has to happen for this form [IB] is ab and c have to be integers, so they have to be whole numbers. b and c can be positive or negative but we also know that a has to be greater than 0. Actually a could also be 0 in case it's a special line. So we know that this first term has to be positive and every other number has to be a integer. Okay, so one example of how this could work is, we have y=-1 half x+7, 6. And we're asked put this in to standard form. Okay, so what we have to do is we first have to make our coefficients whole numbers. Okay, so we need to get rid of all of our fractions. And the way we do that is by multiplying by our least common denominator. So in this case our least common denominator is 6. Multiply everything by 6. That 6 goes to the y, 6 goes to the negative -1 half and to the 7, 6 we're left with +7. Next thing we need to do is make sure our coefficient on x is actually positive. So right now it's positive which means we have to bring it over to the other side. So we add it over, 6y+3x=7, just a little bit of rearranging cause our x term is supposed to be first, 3x+6y=7. These are the exact same line. This one you know is in slope intercept. This one is in standard. And who would be waiting on this damp soggy island...
