Title: How to Change Your DiscordUsername on Android Discord is a popular communication platform that allows users to connect with others through voice, video, and text chat. One common customization that many users make to their Discord account is changing their username. This guide will walk you through the process of changing your Discord username on an Android device. Step 1: Open the Discord App Locate the Discord app on your Android device and tap on it to open. The icon looks like a white game controller on a blue background. If you haven't downloaded the Discord app yet, you can find it on the Google Play Store and install it before proceeding. Step 2: Access User Settings Tap on the hamburger menu (three horizontal lines) located at the top left corner of the screen. From there, scroll down and select 'User Settings.' This section contains all your personal information related to your Discord account, such as profile pictures, usernames, email addresses, etc. Step 3: Select Edit User Profile Once inside User Settings, look for the option labeled 'My Account'. Tapping this option reveals your current account details, including your username. To edit any detail, click on the 'Edit' button next to it. For our purpose here, we want to change our username; therefore, click 'Edit' next to 'username.' Step 4: Enter New Username After clicking 'Edit', a text field becomes available where you can input your new preferred username. Remember, your new username must be unique and cannot exceed 32 characters. Additionally, special characters are allowed but spaces aren't. Once you have entered your desired username, move forward by tapping 'Save.' Key Tips & Guidelines: - Be creative but considerate while choosing a new username. Avoid using offensive or inappropriate language. - Refrain from impersonating someone else or violating anyone's privacy rights. - You may choose to only slightly modify your existing username if you wish to maintain recognition among your contacts. Step 5: Verify Identity Because changing your username has security implications, Discord requires verification via email. Check your inbox for a message from Discord titled "Confirm Email Address." Click the link provided within this email to verify your identity. Note that this confirmation link expires after three minutes, so act quickly! Step 6: Complete Verification Process Upon clicking the verification link sent to your email, you'll be redirected to a page confirming successful completion of the process. Now go back to the Discord app on your Android device. Step 7: Observe Updated Username Navigate back to your User Setting > My Account page. You should now see your newly updated username displayed prominently. Congratulations! You've successfully changed your Discord username on your Android device. Remember: - Changing your username does NOT affect your friend list nor server memberships. All these remain intact post-username change. - However, take note that other users might not recognize you immediately due to your new username. It would help if you informed them about the change proactively. What happened to the boy and the girl in the story? Can you find something on there you like? Title: How to Fix Xbox 360 Error E68 Introduction: The Xbox 360 error code E68 is often associated with issues related to overheating or power supply problems. This guide will walk you through troubleshooting steps to help resolve this issue. Always remember to handle your console carefully and avoid touching any sensitive components directly. **Step 1:** Unplug All Cables from Your Xbox 360 This initial step ensures that there's no residual power left in the system when you begin working on it. It also reduces the risk of electrical shock. **Step 2:** Allow the Console to Cool Down If your Xbox 360 has just been used, give it some time to cool down before proceeding. Overheating can cause various errors including the dreaded E68. Never attempt to speed up this process by blowing air onto the device as this could force dust further into its internal parts. **Step 3:** Check Power Supply Connections Ensure that all cords are securely connected at both ends - from the wall outlet to the power brick, and from the power brick to the console itself. A loose connection might be causing the E68 error. **Step 4:** Examine the Power Brick Indicators Check if the light on the power brick is green. If it's orange or red, it indicates an insufficient power supply which may lead to the E68 error. Try plugging the power brick into a different socket or using a different cable. **Step 5:** Clean Out Dust From The System Dust build-up can hinder ventilation and cause overheating. Use a soft brush or compressed air (held upright) to gently clean away dust accumulated around vents and ports. Avoid spraying liquid near the console. **Step 6:** Reset the Video Settings Sometimes, incorrect video settings can trigger the E68 error. To reset them: 1. Turn off your Xbox 360 completely. 2. Press and hold the sync button (located between the 'LB' and 'RB' buttons) while turning on the console. 3. Release the sync button once the console starts up. Your screen should now display "Setting Up Your Display." Follow the prompts to configure your TV settings. **Step 7:** Perform a Hard Reset A hard reset involves unplugging the console, waiting for about a minute, then pressing the power button several times while reconnecting the power cord. This action drains any remaining power and clears temporary data stored in the RAM, potentially resolving the E68 error. **Step 8:** Test Your Xbox 360 After Each Step After performing each step, turn on your Xbox 360 to see if the problem persists. If so, move on to the next solution. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Regularly check and maintain your Xbox 360's cleanliness to prevent overheating and other malfunctions. * Make sure your console is placed in a well-ventilated area. * Don't block the vents with objects or place items on top of the console. * Keep your software updated to ensure optimal performance. By following these steps meticulously, you significantly increase the chances of fixing the Xbox 360 error E68 without needing professional assistance. However, if the issue continues after trying all suggested solutions, consider contacting Microsoft Support for further assistance. Well if I sit there where we going to put the plates? Differential Equations for Grade Schoolers Have you ever wondered how we can describe things that change over time, like the population of a town or the amount of money in your piggy bank? One way mathematicians do this is through something called differential equations (DEs). A differential equation relates how a quantity changes (the derivative) to the current value of that quantity. Let's explore first order linear DEs, which involve only the first derivative of one variable with respect to another. Imagine driving along a straight road. You measure your speed every second - that's the rate of change of position. Now suppose there is a constant force pushing against you, like wind resistance. That force acts on your speed, trying to slow you down. In mathematical language, you could write this relationship as: speed' + k * speed = initial\_speed Here, "speed'" represents the rate of change of your speed (acceleration), "k" stands for the resisting force, and "initial\_speed" is your starting speed. This type of equation is known as a first order linear DE. Its general solution has the following form: speed = initial\_speed / k + C * e^(-k*t) In this formula, "C" is a constant determined by your specific situation, "e" is a special number around 2.718, and "t" represents time. Let's break down the different parts of this equation using a real-life example: Suppose you start off rollerblading at 6 m/s, and there's a gentle breeze adding 0.5 N of drag (resistance) to each kilogram of your mass. We'll call your total mass "m", meaning k equals 0.5 N/(kg*m) since Force=mass x acceleration: speed = (6 m/s) / (0.5 N/(kg*m)) + C * e^(-(0.5 N/(kg*m)) * t) Simplifying further gives us: speed = 12 kg*m/s + C * e^(-0.5 t/s) Now imagine measuring your speed every second during the first minute. As long as no other forces act upon you, knowing your initial speed allows us to calculate the values of "C" based on those measurements. Once calculated, we can predict future speeds using our general solution! So while complex concepts like electromagnetism and integration may seem daunting now, understanding basic ideas behind differential equations opens doors towards comprehending more advanced topics later on. Keep exploring math, and who knows what exciting adventures await! The basket is being held up by the balloon. Then we could say it's a z sheep. [mm ah] went the little green frog one day. No you have a book like it. Title: How to Change the Lock Screen on Your Mac Introduction: Changing your lock screen on a Mac can be an excellent way to personalize your device and make it stand out. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on changing your Mac's lock screen using built-in features. By following these steps, you can add personality and customization to your computer while ensuring that only authorized users have access. Step 1: Access System Preferences To begin, click on the Apple icon located at the top left corner of your screen. From the dropdown menu, select 'System Preferences.' This action opens the main settings panel where you can manage various aspects of your Mac. Key Tips & Guidelines: The Apple logo is always present on the Menu Bar at the top of the screen. If you ever get lost or need quick access to system settings, this icon serves as a handy shortcut. Make sure no other applications are currently running fullscreen so they do not obscure any part of the System Preferences window when opened. Step 2: Navigate to Desktop & Screen Saver Within the System Preferences window, locate and click on 'Desktop & Screen Saver'. This option allows you to configure both your desktop background and screensaver, including the lock screen image. Step 3: Choose Screensaver Tab Inside the Desktop & Screen Saver pane, there are two tabs - one labeled 'Desktop' (which controls the wallpaper) and another called 'Screen Saver' (managing the lock screen). Click on the latter tab to proceed. Step 4: Select a Screensaver Option The Screen Saver tab presents several categories containing preloaded options provided by macOS. These include 'Random,' 'Classic,' 'Nature,' 'Solar,' etc. Explore each category until you find a suitable screensaver that catches your eye. For more variety, consider downloading third-party screensavers online. Step 5: Customize Display Timeout Settings After selecting a preferred screensaver, adjust the display timeout setting below the preview area. This control determines how long your Mac must remain idle before activating the chosen screensaver. Drag the slider to set the duration according to your preference. Key Tip: A shorter timeout period might help conserve battery life if you primarily use your MacBook away from power sources. Step 6: Set a Hot Corner Shortcut (Optional) Hot Corners offer additional functionality within macOS. To assign a hot corner for instantly triggering the screensaver, click the 'Hot Corners...' button near the bottom right corner of the Screen Saver pane. Then, choose an available corner from the popup menu and associate it with the 'Start Screen Saver' action. Step 7: Enable Require Password After Sleep or Screen Saver Begins For security purposes, ensure that the checkbox next to this option is ticked. Doing so requires entering your user password after waking up your Mac from sleep mode or exiting the activated screensaver. Step 8: Save Changes and Exit System Preferences Once satisfied with all settings, click the 'OK' button at the lower right corner of the Hot Corners dialog box (if open), then press 'Close' at the upper left corner of the Desktop & Screen Saver pane. With these changes saved, your new lock screen should now appear whenever the specified conditions are met. Conclusion: Customizing your Mac's lock screen adds a touch of individuality while enhancing its visual appeal. Following this detailed tutorial ensures a smooth process that respects both aesthetics and security concerns. One day, a big number war happened. In this war, all the numbers wanted to be the best. CONTACT US (800) 457-4255 Once you have thoroughly studied intonation and word connections, you can begin to address the sounds of English. The three most important vowels are [ ], [ ], and . This last symbol, called the schwa, is represented with an upside down e, and is the most common sound in the English language. These are the vowels found in cat, caught and cut. The three most distinctive consonants are R, the American middle T, and the Th sound. The R i s a consonant, but it acts more like a vowel, because the tip of the tongue doesn't touch anywhere in the mouth. The middle T is what makes a word like meeting sound like meeding. As the most commonly used word in English is the word the, the Th is very important. Here are some very high-frequency TH words: the, these, those, they, them, there, they're, their, this, that and then. If these and those are pronounced with a D instead of a TH, it sounds like dese and dose, which is considered lower class in America. The American R The American R is like a vowel because it does not touch anywhere in the mouth. In Korean, Japanese, Spanish, Italian, Greek and many other languages, the R is a consonant because it touches behind the teeth. The American R is produced deep in the throat. Like the French R and the German R, the American R is in the throat, but unlike those two consonant sounds, it doesn't touch. Let's contrast two similar sounds: [ ] and [r]. Hold your hand out in front of you, with your palm up, like you are holding a tray on it. Slightly drop your hand down, and say ah, like you want the doctor to see your throat. Now, curl your fingers up slightly, and say [r]. Your tongue should feel in about the same position as your hand. Let's start with the [ ] sound. Although it's not a common sound, [ ] is very distinctive to the ear, and is typically American. In the practice paragraph vowel chart, this sound occurs 5 times. As its phonetic symbol indicates, [ ] is a combination of [ ] + [e]. To pronounce it, drop your jaw down as if you were going to say [ ]; then from that position, try to say eh. The f inal sound is not two separate vowels, but rather the end result of the combination. It is very close to the sound that a goat makes: ma-a-a-a! If you find yourself getting too nasal with [ ], pinch your nose as you say it. Go to the practice paragraph and find the 5 sounds, including [ u] as in down or out. The [ ] sound is a more common sound than [ ]; you will find 10 such sounds in the practice paragraph. To pronounce [ ], relax your tongue and drop your jaw as far down as it will go. As a matter of fact, put your hand under your chin and say [m ], [p ], [t ], [s ]. Your hand should be pushed down by your jaw as it opens. Remember, it's the sound that you make when the doctor wants to see your throat. Last is the schwa, the most common sound in American English. When you work on the practice paragraph, depending on how fast you speak, how smoothly you make liaisons, how strong your into nation is, how much you relax your sounds, you will find from 50 to 75 schwas. Spelling doesn't help identify it, because it can appear as any one of the vowels, or a combination of them. It is a neutral vowel sound, uh. It is usually in an unstressed syllable, though it can be stressed as well. Whenever you find a vowel that can be crossed out and its absence wouldn't change the pronunciation of the word, you have probably found a schwa: photography [f't gr'fee] (the two apostrophes show the location of the neutral vowel sounds). Because it is so common, however, the wrong pronunciation of this one little sound can leave your speech strongly accented, even if you Americanize everything else. Remember, some dictionaries use two different written characters, the upside down e & [^] for the neutral uh sound, but for simplicity, we are only going to use the first one. Silent or Neutral? A schwa is neutral, but it is not silent. By comparison, the silent E at the end of a word is a signal for pronunciation, but it is not pronounced itself: code is [kod]. The E tells you to say an [o]. If you leave the E off, you have cod, [k d]. The schwa, on the other hand, is neutral, but it is an actual sound, uh. For example, you could also write photography as phuh-tah-gruh-fee. The schwa is a neutral sound, (no distinctive characteristics), but it is the most common sound in the English language. To make the uh sound, put your hand on your diaphragm and push until a grunt escapes. Don't move your jaw, tongue or lips, just allow the sound to flow past your vocal cords. It should sound like uh, not ah. Once you master the two sounds [ ] and uh, you will have an easier time pronouncing 'can' and 'can't'. In a sentence, the simple positive 'can' sound like [k'n]. The simple negative 'can't' sounds like [k n(t)]. The American T The American T is influenced very strongly by intonation and its position in a word or phrase. It can be a little tricky if you try to base your pronunciation on spelling alone. There are, however, 4 basic rules: [T is T], [T is D], [T is Silent],[T is Held]. 1 Top of the Staircase [T is T] If the T is at the beginning of a word (or the top of the staircase), it is a strong, clear T sound. In the beginning of a word: table, take, tomorrow, teach, ten, turn Thomas tried two times. With a stressed T and ST, TS, TR, CT, LT and sometimes NT combinations: They control the contents. In the past tense, D sounds like T, after an unvoiced consonant sound - f, k, p, s, ch, sh, th (but not T). picked [pikt], hoped [houpt], raced [rast], watched [w cht], washed [w sht] It took Tim ten times to try the telephone. 2 Middle of the Staircase [T is D] If the T is in the middle of the word, intonation changes the sound to a soft D. Letter sounds like [ledder]. Water, daughter, bought a, caught a, lot of, got a, later, meeting, better Practice these sentences: What a good idea. [w'd' g d i deey'] Put it in a bottle. [p di di n' b d'l] Get a better water heater. [gedda bedder w der heeder] Put all the data in the computer. [p dall the dayd' in the k'mpyuder] Patty ought to write a better letter. [p dy d' ride a bedder ledder] 3 T is Silent T and N are so close in the mouth that the [t] can disappear. If the T is at the end of a word, you almost don't hear it at all. put, what, lot, set, hot, sit, shot, brought. That's quite right, isn't it? 4 Bottom of the Staircase [T is Held] With -tain, -tten and some TN combinations, the T is held. The "held T" is, strictly speaking, not really a T at all. Remember, [t] and [n] are very close in the mouth. If you have [n] immediately after [t], you don't pop the [t]-the tongue is in the [t] position, but your release the air for the [n] not the [t]. Make sure you don't put a schwa before the [n]. An important point to remember is that you need a sharp upward sliding intonation up to the "held T," then a quick drop for the N. Written, certain, forgotten, sentence He's forgotten the carton of satin mittens. She's certain that he has written it. Martin has gotten a kitten. The American L The American L has two different pronunciations in English (of course, otherwise it would be too easy!). In the beginning or middle of a word, the tongue tip touches just behind the teeth - on those hard ridges. In this position, the L shouldn't give you much trouble. The difficulty begins when the L is at the end of a word. Because the letter L has a shorter, sharper pronunciation in other languages, this will carry over into English, where the whole word will just sound too short. At the end of a word, the L is especially noticeable if it is either missing (Chinese) or too short (Spanish). You need to put a little schwa sound before the final L. If you want to say the word ball, [b l], it will sound too short if you don't say [b -uhl]. You may even need to add a tiny schwa at the end to finish off the L, [b -uh-luh]. One way to avoid the pronunciation difficulty of a final L, such as in call, is to make a liaison when the next word begins with a vowel. For example, if you want to say I have to call on my friend, let the liaison do your work for you; say, [I have to k -l n my friend]. The most common word in the English language is THE, so after the schwa, [th] would be the sound you would hear most often, which is why it is so important to master it. ([th] also exists in English, Greek and Castillian Spanish.) Besides 'the,' there are several other very common words that start with a voiced [th]: this, that, that, those, they, them, their, there, then Just as with most of the other consonants, there are two types - voiced and unvoiced. The voiced TH is like a D, but instead of being in back of the teeth, it's 1/4 inch lower and forward, between the teeth. The unvoiced TH is like an S between the teeth. Most people tend to replace the unvoiced TH with S or T and the voiced one with Z or D. Instead of thing, they say sing, or ting. Instead of that, they say zat or dat. To pronounce TH correctly, think of a snake's tongue. You don't want to take a big relaxed tongue and push it far between your teeth and just leave it out there. Make only a very quick, sharp little movement. Keep the tip of your tongue very tense. It darts out between your teeth and snaps back very quickly. These two sounds probably give you a lot of trouble. One reason for this is that most languages don't make a distinction here. Another reason is that there are four ways of saying these two sounds, depending what the final consonant is. This another place where intonation and pronunciation overlap. When you say the long [e], it is a tense vowel sound. You slightly draw your lips back and raise the back of your tongue. When you say the short [i], it is a lax vowel sound. Don't move your lips AT ALL and open your throat. If the final consonant is unvoiced (whispered), [t, k, f, p, s, sh, ch], then the middle vowel sound is quick and sharp: [bit] or [beet] If the final consonant is voiced (spoken), [d, g, v, b, z, zh, j] or any vowel, then the middle vowel sound is doubled: [bi-id] or [bee-eed]. let's put this on the back here like this... In between the ribs were sloping surfaces of stone or brick that could be much thinner and lighter than the ribs. At first the pattern made by the ribs was quite plain, like Romanesque vaults, but architects, particularly in England, soon started adding small ribs in between the main ones and making different patterns. Some vaults like this can also be seen in Spain and Germany, but not usually in France or Italy. One of the other kids was kind of wild. Perhaps it's a basketball instead of a football. You going to go to the hospital and see him? Photo: buck82 (flickr) Tsunamis are powerful waves caused by underwater earthquakes or eruptions that can crash over ten stories tall into a coastline. If you were sailing far from shore, how would you feel about a tremendous tsunami heading your way? Actually, you wouldn't notice a tsunami at all. In deep water, a tsunami raises the ocean's surface only about one or two feet-much less than ordinary ocean waves. What's more, it takes up to an hour for a single tsunami to roll by. How can a wave be so deadly to a coastline, but so tame in the open ocean? The energy of a wave has more to do with how deep the water churns than how tall the wave rises. Ordinary waves can be rather tall, but they don't go very deep. What's more there's never much distance between one ordinary wave's crest and the one following it. The distance between waves is called "wavelength," and it's another secret of a tsunami's strength. While ordinary waves only tug at the surface water, tsunamis churn the water all the way to the deepest ocean floor! They also have very long wavelengths-a hundred miles or more. Although tsunamis raise the surface only a foot or so in deep water, they carry a lot of energy. When a tsunami approaches a coastline, the water gets shallower. This causes friction at the front of the wave and slows it down. The faster moving back of the wave piles into the slower front, squeezing the whole hundred mile tsunami like an accordion. Just as a rug will crumple and rise if it's scrunched by an opening door, the tsunami gets taller. By the time it hits the beach, it can be a hundred feet high! He had two older brothers called Abel Joseph Hugo (1798-1855) and Eug ne Hugo (1800-1837). He was born in 1802, in Besan on (in the Doubs department). Hugo lived in France for most of his life. During the reign of Napoleon III he went into exile. In 1851, he lived in Belgium, in Brussels.He moved to Jersey in 1852. He stayed there until 1855 when he went to live in Guernsey until 1870. He lived there again in 1872-1873. Title: How to Connect a MIDI Keyboard to Multiplayer Piano Introduction: Multiplayer Piano is an online platform that allows multiple users to play piano together in real-time from their own devices. To enhance your experience, connecting a MIDI keyboard can provide better control and more realistic gameplay. This tutorial will guide you through the process of setting up your MIDI keyboard with Multiplayer Piano. Step 1: Choose Your MIDI Keyboard Before proceeding, ensure you have a compatible MIDI keyboard. Most standard MIDI keyboards should work fine, as long as they are class-compliant USB MIDI controllers or have a USB-to-MIDI interface. Check your keyboard's manual if needed. Key Tip: A MIDI keyboard without built-in sounds only sends data; therefore, choosing one with good build quality, responsive keys, and pitch bend/modulation wheels enhances your playing experience. Step 2: Install Necessary Drivers (if applicable) If using a non-class-compliant USB MIDI controller or a separate USB-to-MIDI interface, install any necessary drivers provided by the manufacturer before connecting them to your computer. Class-compliant devices do not require additional driver installation. Step 3: Plug in Your MIDI Keyboard Using an available USB port, connect your MIDI keyboard to your device (computer, laptop, tablet). If required, plug in external power sources for your keyboard at this time. Step 4: Confirm Connection After physically connecting the keyboard, wait for your operating system to recognize the new hardware. On Windows, check Device Manager for proper identification. MacOS users can open Audio MIDI Setup under Applications > Utilities. The connected keyboard should appear within these applications. Step 5: Open Multiplayer Piano Navigate to <http://www.multiplayerpiano.com> using your preferred web browser. Click 'Play Now', create an account, log in, and enter the virtual room where you wish to participate in collaborative music making. Step 6: Access Settings Within Multiplayer Piano Click the gear icon located near the top right corner of the screen to access settings. Under the 'Input Devices' section, search for your MIDI keyboard model among the listed input options. Key Guideline: Some browsers may take longer than others to detect newly added MIDI devices due to various factors like security settings and permissions. Refresh your page or reconnect the keyboard if necessary. Step 7: Select Your MIDI Keyboard As Input In cases where there are multiple detected MIDI inputs, choose the appropriate one corresponding to your keyboard model. Once selected, confirm changes and exit the settings menu. Step 8: Test Functionality Begin pressing keys on your physical MIDI keyboard while observing the visual feedback on the Multiplayer Piano website. Play notes, chords, switch octaves, activate sustain pedals, etc., ensuring all functions operate smoothly. Step 9: Adjust Latency (Optional) Should latency issues arise during testing, return to settings, find the 'Latency Compensation' option, and adjust accordingly until optimal performance is achieved. Note that higher values increase compensation but might introduce unwanted delay between action and sound output. Conclusion: By following this comprehensive tutorial, you have successfully connected your MIDI keyboard to Multiplayer Piano, providing enhanced interaction and enjoyment when engaging with other musicians across the globe. Remember always to test connections thoroughly and make necessary adjustments for best results. Happy playing! Calculating the Product-Moment Correlation Coefficient The product-moment correlation coefficient, often denoted as 'r', is a statistical measure that indicates the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two variables. This tutorial will guide you through the process of calculating the product-moment correlation coefficient by hand using the following steps: 1. **Understand the Data** Before starting any calculations, ensure that your data meets the assumptions required for Pearson's r (product-moment correlation coefficient). These include having interval or ratio level measurement for both variables, normality within each group, and a linear relationship between the pairs of observations. 2. **Compute Means and Deviations** Calculate the mean (average) for each variable. Then calculate the deviation from the mean for each score. The formula for this is Xi - Mx where Xi represents individual scores and Mx is the mean value of x. Repeat this process for y (Yi - My). 3. **Multiply Paired Deviations Together** For every pair of deviations calculated above, multiply them together. Store these products; they are essential components for later stages. 4. **Sum Up All Products** Add up all the resulting products from Step 3. Label this sum as ' (xy)'. 5. **Square Each Value of X and Y Individually** Square each original raw score for both sets of data (Xi^2, Yi^2). 6. **Sum Up Squared Values** Add up all squared values obtained in Step 5 separately for each dataset. Label these sums as (x^2), (y^2). 7. **Determine N** Count the total number of paired observations in your datasets. Denote this count as 'N'. 8. **Compute Sum of Squares of Deviations About Mean** Using the same deviations computed in Step 2, square each one again but now sum up these squared deviations rather than multiplying them. So, do (xi)^2 and (yi)^2 individually. 9. **Find Variance for Both Sets** Variance can be calculated using the formula Var(x)= (xi)^2/(N-1) and similarly for var(y). Compute variance for both your datasets. 10. **Calculate Standard Deviations** Standard deviation (SD) is simply the square root of variance. Therefore SD(x)= [Var(x)] and SD(y)= [Var(y)]. 1 [dibble dibble DOP DOP DOP]. It looks a bit like a dog. How many presents did Morty and Perdie bring? I want' 'a listen to another one. Step3. structure(about 15 minutes) On board structure--------------as adjective as a. Teacher attaches some pictures on the board( 2 boys of the same height , two hens of the same weight, 2 girls of the same age , two planes of the same speed ..) b. Teacher points to each pair of pictures and says sentences about them. T: look at these two boys Ahmad and Amir.Ahmad is 150cm tall and Amir is also 150 cm tall. T: look at these two hens. This hen is 5 kilos and that hen is also 5 kilos, and so on . in this way the teacher compares each two pictures. c. students look and listen d. Teacher starts from the first pair of pictures and says sentences using as adjective as T: Ahmad is 150cm tall. Amir is 150cm tall. So we can say : Ahmad is as tall as Amir. T: This hen is as fat as that hen. T: This flower is as beautiful as that hen. Teacher says sentences like the above all the pictures. e. Teacher asks the class questions about the pictures. T: Is Amir as tall as Ahmad. S. yes, Amir is as tall as Ahmad. T: Is this brown plane as fast as the red plane is as fast as the red plane. Teacher may ask individuals to answer her questions. D. Teacher writes the rule on the board. Noun + be( am / is / are / was / were ) + as + noun E. Teacher may write several sentences obeying this rule . ( depending on the time of the class ). F. Teacher writes 2 sentences using the same rule to be changed by the adjectives and nouns (pronounces) given. On board Bob is as young as his friend. 1. fat 2. fast 3. clever 4. thin 5. good 6. tall Parvin is as clever as Maryam. 1. I 2.he 3.we 4.she 5. you 6.They 7. my sister Step 4 . Structure On board structure 2 adjective er + than .. a. Teacher attaches some pairs of pictures on one side of the board(2 trees one taller than the other , two women one younger than the other , two cars one newer than the other ..) b. Teacher points to each pair of pictures one by one and says sentences about them. T:look at these two trees. The first tree is 7 meter tall and the second tree is 4.5 meters tall. Look at pair and Zahra. Zahra is 30 years old and Pari is 25 years old. In this way. She compares each two pictures. C. Students look and listen. d. T: so, the first tree is taller than the second tree. T: Zahra is 30 and pari is 25. T: Zahra is older than pari .or pari is younger than Zahra. Teacher says sentences like the above about all pictures. C. Teacher asks students questions about the same pictures. T: Is the first tree taller than the second tree? Rationale: This lesson teaches children about the long vowel correspondence ee = /E/. In this lesson children will learn to recognize, spell, and read words containing the spelling ee. They will learn a meaningful representation, they will spell and read words containing this spelling in a Letterbox lesson, and read a decodable book that focuses on the correspondence ee=/E/. Materials: Primary paper and pencil; letter boxes, letter tiles b, c, d, e, e, g, h, n, p, r, s, t, t, Sheep in a Jeep for all students; word cards with BEE, TREE, DEEP, PEER, TEETH, GREEN, BREED, CREEP, and SCREEN, construction paper and crayons, glue, scissors, assessment worksheet identifying pictures with /E/. 1. Say: In order to become expert readers we need to learn the code that tells us how to pronounce words. Today we are going to learn about the long E sound. When I say /E/ I think of a girl shrieking when she sees a mouse. Now let's look at the spelling of /E/ that we'll learn today. One way to spell /E/ is ee. 2. Say: Before we learn about the spelling of /E/, we need to listen for it in some words. When I listen for /E/ in words, I hear e say its name /E/ and the corners of mouth come up like I'm smiling. I'll show you first: bleed. I heard e say its name and I felt the corners of my mouth come up. There is a long E in bleed. Now I'm going to see if it's in pen. Hmm, I didn't hear e say its name and my mouth didn't smile. Now you try. If you hear /E/ shriek like you just saw a mouse. If you don't hear /E/ say, "That's not it." Do you hear the /E/ sound in: SLEEP, BED, KEEP, PET, SLEEP, CHEESE, WEB? 3. What if I want to spell the word beep? "The microwave made a beep." To spell beep in letterboxes, first I need to know how many phonemes I have in the word so I stretch it out and count: /b/ /ee/ /p/. I need 3 boxes. I heard that /E/ just before the /p/ so I'm going to put the ee in the 2nd box. The word starts with /b/, I need an b in the 1st box. The ending sound is /p/ so I'm going to put a p in the last box. Now I'll show you how I would read a tough word. [Display poster with speech on the top and model reading the word.] I'm going to start with the ee; that part says /E/. Now I'm going to put the beginning letters with it: s-p-ee. Now I'll put that chunk together with the last sound, /ch/. Oh, speech, like "The President gave a speech." 4. Say: Now I'm going to have you spell some words in letterboxes. Word list: 2 - [bee], 3--[tree, deep, peer, teeth, hen], 4--[green, breed, creep], and 5--[screen]. 5. Say: Now I am going to let you read the words you've spelled. [Have children read words in unison. Afterwards, call on individuals to read one word on the list until everyone has had a turn.] 6. Say: You've done a great job and reading words with our new spelling for /E/: ee. Now we are going to read a book called Sheep in a Jeep. In this story five sheep get into a jeep and get into some trouble. Let's read to see what kind of trouble these sheep get into. [Children pair up and take turns reading alternate pages each while teacher walks around the room monitoring progress. After individual paired reading, the class rereads Sheep in a Jeep aloud together, and stops between page turns to discuss the plot.] 7. Do beginning constants worksheet. Say: On this worksheet we want to color the objects that have the /E/ sound. 8. Say: Before we finish up with our lesson about one way to spell /E/ = ee, let's see if we can find ee words in the story we just read. Everyone will make a sheep out of construction paper and then write the long E words that were in Sheep in a Jeep on the sheep. Dr. Murray, Bruce. The Reading Genie, Mouth Moves and Gestures for Phonemes. http://www.auburn.edu/academic/education/reading_genie/mouthmoves.html Beginning Constants Worksheet. Shaw, Nancy E. Sheep in a Jeep. Title: Embrace Your Individuality: Be Happy About Being Unpopular and People Calling You Ugly Introduction: In our society, there is often immense pressure to fit in with popular trends and maintain an image that aligns with societal standards of beauty. However, it's essential to remember that individuality should be celebrated, and being unpopular or labeled as 'ugly' does not define your worth or happiness. This wiki-style guide will help you embrace your unique qualities, develop self-confidence, and find joy in your own skin. Step 1: Understand That Popularity Is Subjective Popularity varies across different social circles and communities. What one group finds appealing may not resonate with another. Moreover, popularity can be influenced by factors beyond personal control, such as socioeconomic status, family background, and physical appearance. Recognizing this subjectivity allows you to detach from the need for external validation and focus on developing genuine connections based on shared interests and values. Key Tip: Focus on building meaningful relationships rather than seeking superficial acceptance. Step 2: Reflect on Your Personal Values and Interests Take time to explore activities and ideas that genuinely interest you, regardless of whether they are considered 'cool' or mainstream. Engaging in these pursuits will enable you to meet like-minded individuals who appreciate you for who you are and provide opportunities for growth and fulfillment. Guideline: Set aside dedicated time each week to engage in hobbies, join clubs, attend events, or volunteer within your community. Step 3: Develop Self-Acceptance and Confidence Cultivating self-acceptance starts by acknowledging your strengths and weaknesses without judgment. Practice positive affirmations, focusing on what makes you special and valuable. Surround yourself with supportive friends and loved ones who uplift and encourage you. Key Tip: Create a list of your best attributes and refer back to it regularly when feelings of doubt or insecurity arise. Step 4: Ignore Negative Comments and Stereotypes Understand that people's opinions about you do not determine your worth. When faced with derogatory remarks or stereotypes, remind yourself that everyone has their biases and preferences. Instead of internalizing others' criticisms, concentrate on improving your mental wellbeing and fostering healthy relationships. Guideline: Implement a 'gray rock' strategy - respond minimally and neutrally to negative comments, avoiding engagement or justification. Step 5: Challenge Societal Norms and Expectations Challenge conventional norms surrounding beauty and popularity by promoting body positivity and inclusiveness. Support organizations advocating for equal representation and rights for all individuals, irrespective of their appearance or social standing. Key Tip: Use social media platforms to amplify diverse voices and perspectives while sharing your experiences and insights. Step 6: Cultivate Empathy and Compassion Towards Others By practicing kindness towards others, we foster understanding and connection. Show empathy towards those struggling with similar issues and offer support where possible. Building a strong support network enables us to navigate challenging situations more effectively. Guideline: Participate in community outreach programs or initiatives focused on promoting unity and solidarity among marginalized groups. Conclusion: Embracing your uniqueness and finding happiness despite societal pressures requires resilience, introspection, and self-compassion. By following these steps, you can cultivate a sense of inner peace and contentment rooted in authenticity and self-love. Remember, true beauty lies within, and being unpopular or labeled as 'ugly' does not diminish your value or potential for happiness. Everybody has got clam chowder to start with? For many centuries, the celebration of Christmas often began with a church service or mass, which lasted from late at night to after midnight on Christmas morning. Christmas Day was a time of feasting. On the following day, the Feast of Saint Stephen people from rich households would carry boxes of food out to the street for the poor and hungry. Many people would go back to work but employers would give gifts of money to their workers. The Holy Days continued with the feast of Saint John and Holy Innocents' Day. The feasting and parties ended on the Feast of the Epiphany, the day of the Three Wise Men, often called the "Three Kings". The season is nowadays remembered by the song "The Twelve Days of Christmas". William Shakespeare wrote a play to be performed as part of the celebration, called "Twelfth Night". Dealing with fireworks when you have PTSD can be challenging, but there are strategies that can help minimize your discomfort and anxiety. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you cope: ### Step 1: Understand Your Triggers Before firework season arrives, take some time to reflect on what specifically triggers your PTSD symptoms. Is it the sound, the sight, or the unpredictability of fireworks? Knowing your triggers will allow you to prepare accordingly and implement effective coping mechanisms. **Key Tip:** Keep a journal to document your reactions to past firework displays. This will provide valuable insight into your personal triggers. ### Step 2: Plan Ahead Once you know your triggers, create a plan for managing them during firework seasons. Discuss your concerns with friends, family, or support networks so they can assist you if needed. **Guideline:** Consider informing neighbors about your situation; they may be willing to notify you before setting off any fireworks. ### Step 3: Choose a Safe Space Identify a safe space in your home where you can retreat during firework events. Ideally, this should be a quiet room with minimal windows and good insulation to muffle outside noise. **Key Tip:** Prepare this space ahead of time by adding comfort items such as blankets, pillows, calming music, or white noise machines. ### Step 4: Utilize Relaxation Techniques Practice relaxation techniques like deep breathing exercises, progressive muscle relaxation, or guided imagery to calm yourself during stressful moments. These methods can help reduce hyperarousal and promote feelings of safety and control. **Guideline:** Start practicing these techniques well before firework season starts so you feel comfortable using them when necessary. ###Step 5: Use Noise-Canceling Headphones or Earplugs Invest in high-quality noise-canceling headphones or earplugs to block out loud noises. Pair them with calming sounds or music to further distract from distressing stimuli. **Key Tip:** Test different types of headphones or earplugs to find what works best for you. Some people prefer over-ear headphones, while others find earbuds more comfortable. ### Step 6: Implement Grounding Strategies Grounding techniques can help keep you present and focused during overwhelming situations. Try activities like holding ice cubes, focusing on physical sensations, or repeating a mantra. **Guideline:** Practice grounding exercises regularly to build up their effectiveness. ### Step 7: Seek Professional Help If fireworks significantly impact your quality of life, consider seeking professional help from a mental health provider. They can offer additional coping skills, therapy, or medication to manage your PTSD symptoms. **Key Tip:** Look for therapists who specialize in trauma-focused treatments such as Prolonged Exposure (PE), Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT), or Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR). Remember, dealing with fireworks when you have PTSD is an individual process. What works for one person might not work for another. Be patient with yourself and continue exploring new strategies until you find those that provide relief. hm, he [laughing] tries to help Mathilda out and what happens? Force a Career of Your Choice on The Sims 3 PC (Including Cheats) The Sims 3 is a popular life simulation game that allows players to create and control virtual people, known as "Sims," in a variety of careers. However, sometimes you might want your Sim to pursue a specific career path that isn't readily available or they aren't qualified for. This tutorial will guide you through the process of forcing a career of your choice on The Sims 3 using cheats, with clear explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Enable Cheats** To begin, you need to enable cheats in The Sims 3. Here's how: 1. While playing the game, press `Ctrl + Shift + C` on your keyboard simultaneously to open the cheat console. 2. Type 'testingcheatsenabled true' without quotes and hit Enter. You should see a message saying "Cheats are enabled." Enabling cheats allows you to bypass certain limitations within the game, making it possible to force a career of your choice. **Step 2: Set Careers** Now, let's set up the desired career for your Sim. Follow these steps: 1. Open the cheat console again by pressing `Ctrl + Shift + C`. 2. Type 'careers.promote <career>' replacing "<career>" with the name of the career you wish your Sim to have (e.g., 'careers.promote Astronaut'). If the career has multiple branches, add '.branch<number>' after the career name (e.g., 'careers.promote Business branch1' for the Investor branch). Hit Enter. 3. Repeat Step 2 until your Sim reaches level 10 in their chosen career. This method forces your Sim into any career you desire while also giving them instant promotions. It skips the qualifications required for those promotions, so keep this in mind when choosing your Sim's new profession. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * **Careful with Skill Building**: Since skill requirements are often tied to job performance, consider building relevant skills if you want your Sim to perform well at work. For instance, Logic and Charisma can help a Sim excel in the Science and Politics careers, respectively. * **Manage Time Effectively**: Promoting your Sim directly to level 10 may cause them to miss out on important rewards from lower levels. Make sure to check what benefits come with each promotion and adjust accordingly. * **Monitor Moodlets**: Certain moodlets can impact your Sim's job performance. Keep an eye on their emotional state before sending them off to work. * **Experiment**: Don't be afraid to try different combinations of careers and promotions - part of the fun lies in discovering unique ways to develop your Sims! By following this detailed tutorial, you now know how to force a career of your choice on The Sims 3 using cheats. This is the first post I've had since September and I had to share these cool multiplication disks. My students needed to practice their multiplication facts so I told them I would make them flash cards. However, to make flash cards for multiplication and division facts 1 through 12 for 17 children... well that's a lot of paper. So I found these disks and they took far less time, and are so much cooler. My kiddo's loved them! So here is how to make them: 1. Cut out a circle. 2. Put 12 holes around the edge - I used a cool star punch. 3. Put x ___ (the factor i.e. 1,2,3,4 etc...) in the middle. 4. Next to each hole write the numbers 1- 12 (I wrote mine random - because I wanted my students to practice them - you could also write them in order) 1. Write __(same factor as above) 2. Put your finger over the hole and figure out the problem from the other side. For example: If your factor in the middle is 6 and the hole you put your finger over is 7 (from the multiplication side) do 6 x 7. This equals 42 so write 42 on the hole. Do this for each number. Here is an example of step two from above: 6 x 10 is equal to 60. When I flip the disk over and look at the number where my finger was it should show 60 This is such a cool tool to use in the classroom... but so hard to explain... let me know if I need to upload a video and I'll see what I can do. Today, let's talk about sets and something called "ranks." You might already know about sets - they are just collections of things. Like your collection of toy cars or your deck of cards. In mathematics, we often deal with sets of numbers or shapes. Now, imagine organizing all these sets into big boxes. Each box contains smaller boxes, which themselves contain even smaller boxes, and so on. This forms a kind of tower of sets. We start with a small box labeled $V\_0,$ which is just an empty box. Then, we create a new box $V\_1$ containing everything we could put in a box using our first box $V\_0.$ That means $V\_1$ has one thing in it - an empty box! Next, we make another box $V\_2$ that includes everything we could fit in a box using both $V\_0$ and $V\_1.$ Since $V\_1$ only had one item, $V\_2$ also doesn't have many items. But watch what happens when we keep making more boxes - eventually, we get boxes that hold lots of other boxes! So where does the concept of "rank" come in? Well, every box has a certain size or position compared to other boxes. We call this its rank. To find the rank of any set or box, follow these steps: 1. Check whether the set is inside a box like $V\_{1}, V\_{2}$ or $V\_{3}.$ Make sure there isn't a smaller box it fits in instead. 2. Once you find the smallest box it belongs to, add 1 to the number labeling that box. And voila! There's the rank of your set. For example, the rank of our good old friend $V\_0$ (the empty box) is actually 0 because it lives in $V\_1$ (which makes its rank 1) but is too small to live anywhere else. Neat, huh? Remember though, no set can ever belong in a box with the same label as itself or lower; otherwise, we wouldn't have found the smallest box possible! Let me leave you with one final thought: ranks give us order among sets. Just like knowing who goes first in line or alphabetical order helps avoid chaos, understanding ranks keeps our mathematical world nice and organized. Happy counting, future set-explorers! Catty was so happy, she felt like she was flying too! As they flew, Catty looked down and saw all the beautiful flowers and trees in the garden. She knew that one day, she would have wings of her own and fly even higher. And when that day came, Catty became a beautiful butterfly. She thanked Birdy for helping her try to fly before. Title: How to Breed Minecraft Horses Introduction: Minecraft horses can be bred to produce foals with better attributes such as speed, jump height, and health. This tutorial will guide you through the process of breeding horses in Minecraft, providing detailed steps and useful tips along the way. Step 1: Finding Horses First, locate two adult horses. They can be found naturally spawned in plains or savannah biomes. You can identify them by their tall stature and horse-like appearance. Once you have found two suitable horses, proceed to Step 2. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Make sure both horses are adults; baby horses (foals) cannot be bred. * Ideally, find horses with high stats (indicated by gold stars above their health bar when selecting them). Higher stats will result in stronger offspring. Step 2: Taming Horses Tame both horses before attempting to breed them. Approach an untamed horse and mount it. The horse may buck you off repeatedly until its temperament meter is filled up completely, indicating that it has been tamed successfully. Repeat this process for the second horse if necessary. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Feeding the horse sugar, wheat, apples, or golden carrots while trying to tame it will speed up the taming process. * Be patient during the taming process, as it might take some time depending on your luck. Step 3: Leading Horses Once both horses are tamed, attach a lead to each of them and bring them close together so they stand near each other but do not overlap. Step 4: Breeding Horses To initiate breeding, feed each tamed horse either a Golden Apple or enchanted Golden Carrot. To craft an enchanted Golden Carrot, combine a regular Golden Carrot with a level 1 or higher Enchantment Book using an Anvil. After feeding both horses, hearts will appear above their heads, signifying successful mating. A short period later, a baby horse (foal) will emerge from between the parents. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Using enchanted Golden Carrots increases the chances of producing a foal with higher base stats compared to using plain Golden Apples. * Keep in mind that breeding consumes food items from your inventory, so ensure you have enough resources before starting. Step 5: Waiting for Foal Maturity After the birth of the foal, allow it to grow into adulthood by waiting about twenty minutes (real-time) or five Minecraft days. During this growth phase, keep the foal safe from any potential threats like hostile mobs. Step 6: Separating Parents Separate the parent horses after breeding to prevent immediate subsequent pregnancies. This allows you to control the number of horses in your area and maintain space for new foals to grow without overcrowding. Conclusion: By following these six simple steps, you now know how to breed Minecraft horses effectively. Remember always to consider the wellbeing and safety of your horses while managing their population size. Enjoy raising your equine companions and exploring the vast world of Minecraft! [Chi runs pencil along long line of words] Title: How to Draw a Lambda Symbol Introduction: The lambda symbol ( ) is a letter from the Greek alphabet that has various uses in mathematics, science, and engineering. It can represent different concepts depending on the field, such as wavelength in physics or anonymity in computer programming. This tutorial will guide you through drawing this versatile symbol with precision and ease. By following these steps, you'll be able to create your own lambda symbols for personal notes, study materials, or presentations. Step 1: Familiarize yourself with the lambda symbol Before starting, take a moment to observe the shape and proportions of the lambda symbol. The uppercase version looks like a slanted capital 'A', while the lowercase resembles a reversed 'n'. Note the symmetry along the vertical axis. Understanding its structure will help you draw it accurately. Step 2: Choose the size and orientation Decide whether you want to draw the symbol horizontally or vertically. If drawing horizontally, consider which direction you prefer - left-to-right (like English text) or right-to-left (opposite of English). For beginners, we recommend drawing from left to right. Once decided, determine the height and width based on where you plan to use the symbol. A common height range is between 0.5 to 1 inch (1.3 to 2.5 cm), but adjust according to your needs. Step 3: Sketch two parallel lines Using a ruler if necessary, lightly sketch two evenly spaced horizontal lines serving as guides for the crossbar of the lambda. Make sure they are parallel and equidistant throughout their length. These should occupy about one third of the total height measured in Step 2, leaving space above and below them. Key Tip: Keeping these lines light ensures easy erasure later without affecting the final product. Step 4: Draw the top diagonal stroke Start at the far left end of the top guideline and gently slope downwards towards the right until reaching the bottom guideline. Maintain consistency in angle and curvature by imagining a straight line connecting both ends before actually drawing it. Be careful not to press too hard; maintain a consistent pressure similar to when writing with a pen or pencil. Step 5: Complete the second diagonal stroke From the point where the first stroke intersects the bottom guideline, continue diagonally upwards to meet the top guideline. Again, visualize a smooth curve connecting the start and endpoint before committing to paper. Step 6: Erase unnecessary guidelines Once satisfied with your strokes, carefully erase the horizontal guidelines drawn earlier. Leave only the visible portions of the diagonal strokes. Step 7: Refine the lambda symbol Check for any irregularities in angles, curves, or alignment. Correct them by retracing over the affected areas until achieving a clean, symmetrical lambda symbol. Remember to keep uniform pressure during this process. Guidelines & Key Tips: - Practice consistently: Like any skill, practice makes perfect. Regularly practicing drawing the lambda symbol will improve muscle memory and speed. - Use grid paper: For those struggling with proportion and alignment, using graph paper may provide additional guidance. - Compare against reference: Whenever unsure about accuracy, compare your work against printed or digital references until confident enough to freehand. - Experiment with styles: Try out different font types, weights, and sizes once familiar with basic construction techniques. For your Key Stage 3 science exams you should know that acids have a pH of less than 7. The pH scale is a measure of how concentrated the hydrogen ions are in a solution. Acids with a low pH (nearest to 1) are very acid. Your stomach makes hydrochloric acid: the pH in your stomach should be about pH 2. It has to be very acid to digest the protein in your food because pepsin (the enzyme in your stomach) works best at pH 2. Vinegar is also an acid, but it is a very weak acid. Do you put acid on your food? Probably. The pH scale goes from 1 to 14. Any chemical which has a pH above 7 is the opposite of an acid, we call these chemicals alkalis.Any chemical which releases hydrogen ions when dissolved in water is said to be an acid.Find out about alkalis which are the opposite of acids. Find out about indicators and how to make your own "litmus paper". I hope that you will be able to remember most of the facts on this page, but you must memorise the definition of acids. Write it on a small index card. Put the red words on one side of the card and the blue ones on the other. Add the card to your revision pack. Acids are chemicals which release hydrogen ions when dissolved in water. They have a pH of less than SEVEN. Find out more about the pH scale from my GCSE page. 'Cause that one goes in the book. [S protests a bit; Dad wipes S] Title: How to Choose an Orthopedic Dog Bed Introduction: An orthopedic dog bed is designed to provide extra support and comfort for your furry friend, especially those with joint problems or older dogs. It can help alleviate pain, improve mobility, and promote better sleep. However, choosing the right one might be overwhelming due to the variety of options available. This tutorial will guide you through the process of selecting the perfect orthopedic dog bed for your pet. Step 1: Measure Your Dog The first step in choosing an orthopedic dog bed is measuring your dog. This ensures that the bed is large enough for them to stretch out and turn around comfortably. To measure, note down the length from the nose to the base of the tail and add about 6-12 inches depending on their size. Also, consider their width when they lay down stretched out. Key Tip: If your dog likes curling up while sleeping, add less extra space; if they sprawl out, allow more room. Step 2: Determine the Type of Support Needed Different dogs require different levels of support based on age, weight, and health conditions. For instance, senior dogs and overweight pets typically need more cushioning than younger, leaner ones. Look for beds with high-density foam or memory foam which molds to the shape of the body providing customized support. Guideline: As a general rule, choose a firm yet comfortable bed as too soft a surface may exacerbate existing joint issues by sinking into unnatural positions. Step 3: Check Materials Used Orthopedic dog beds come in various materials like nylon, canvas, microfiber, etc., each having its pros and cons. Opt for durable, easy-to-clean materials that are also resistant to chewing and scratching. The cover should ideally be removable for machine washing. Additional Information: Some covers have waterproof linings making cleaning easier and preventing odors buildup. Step 4: Consider Special Features Some orthopedic dog beds offer additional features such as bolsters (raised edges) for added head and neck support, non-slip bottoms to keep the bed stable, and heating or cooling properties to regulate temperature. Assess what would benefit your pet most according to their habits and needs. Important Note: While raised edges can be beneficial for some dogs who enjoy resting their heads, others might feel trapped. Always observe your pet's preferences before buying. Step 5: Compare Prices & Brands Once you know exactly what you're looking for, start comparing prices across brands. Remember, expensive doesn't always mean better. Read customer reviews, check warranty periods, after-sales service, and return policies before finalizing your purchase. Caution: Be wary of extremely cheap options as these could lack proper support or durability. Conclusion: Selecting the ideal orthopedic dog bed involves careful consideration of several factors including size, type of support needed, material used, special features, and cost. By following this comprehensive guide, you'll ensure that your beloved companion gets the utmost care and comfort they deserve. Look in here and see who else can come to your party. [??? Title: How to Change Clothes in Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas Grand Theft Auto (GTA) San Andreas is an action-adventure game that offers players the freedom to explore its open world and complete various missions. One interesting feature in the game allows players to customize their characters' appearances by changing clothes. This tutorial will guide you through the process of changing clothes in GTA San Andreas, providing clear steps and helpful tips along the way. Step 1: Access Your Safehouse To change your character's clothing, you first need to access a safehouse where your wardrobe is located. A few examples of locations with safehouses are Los Santos, San Fierro, and Las Venturas. Once you have purchased or entered a safehouse, proceed to Step 2. Key Tips & Guidelines: - You can only save outfits at specific safehouses; make sure to purchase them before attempting to change your clothes. - Each region has different available clothing items based on local fashion trends. Explore all regions to unlock more options! Step 2: Enter the Wardrobe Room After entering your safehouse, locate the room containing the wardrobe. Typically, this would be a bedroom or bathroom. Interact with the wardrobe by walking up to it and pressing the appropriate button ("A" on Xbox, "X" on PlayStation, or left-click on PC). Key Tips & Guidelines: - Some safehouses may contain multiple wardrobes; experiment with each one to find new clothing options. - Make sure no other NPCs (Non-Player Characters) are nearby when trying to enter the wardrobe menu, as they might interrupt your actions. Step 3: Browse Available Outfits Once inside the wardrobe menu, navigate using the arrow keys or controller buttons until you reach the 'Outfits' tab. Here, you will see several pre-made outfits which include tops, bottoms, shoes, and accessories. Scroll through these outfits to preview how they look on your character. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Experiment with different combinations of tops, bottoms, shoes, and accessories to create unique styles. - Pay attention to weather conditions while choosing outfits; certain clothing items offer better protection against rain or heat. Step 4: Select an Outfit When you have found an outfit you like, highlight it and press the appropriate button ('A' on Xbox, 'X' on PlayStation, or left-click on PC) to equip it. Confirm your selection by selecting 'Yes' if prompted. Step 5: Save Your New Outfit (Optional) If you want to keep the current combination of clothing items as a separate outfit, navigate to the 'Save/Load Outfit' tab within the wardrobe menu. Highlight an empty slot and press the corresponding button ('A' on Xbox, 'X' on PlayStation, or left-click on PC) to save your new outfit. Give it a memorable name so you can easily find it later. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Saving outfits allows you to quickly switch between different styles without having to manually select each item every time. - Be mindful of saved outfit slots; there is a limited number available depending on your progress in the game. By following these steps, you should now be able to successfully change clothes and create new outfits for your character in GTA San Andreas. Remember to take advantage of regional variations in fashion trends and don't hesitate to mix and match clothing items for unique styles! It's hard to build on the rug with blocks to make them stay. Humidity and Dew Point Humidity refers to the amount of moisture in the air. You might be surprised to know that the air in our atmosphere has water in it. Lots of water! Water in the atmosphere exists in 3 main states. 2.CLOUD DROPLETS (SOMETIMES FROZEN ICE CRYSTALS) 3.LIQUID RAIN DROPS (SOMETIMES FROZEN!) Air near the ground or ocean usually has more water in it than the colder air up high. Measuring the Amount of Water Vapor in the Air: There are several different ways to measure and to express the amount of water in the air. Lets imagine we have a blob of air with moisture in it. The temperature of our blob of air is 80 degrees. So, how do we tell how much moisture is in it ???? The relative humidity tells how much water the air is holding compared to how much it could hold at a certain temperature. If our blob of air has a relative humidity of 50% then that means it is holding half of the amount of water a blob of air 80 degrees could hold. The relative humidity can change if the moisture changes or if the temperature changes. The dew point is a much better indicator of moisture in the air and is preferred by most meteorologists. What is the Dew Point? The dew point is the temperature at which the air will be holding all the moisture it can if cooled. Or...another way of putting it. The dew point is the temperature at which the relative humidity reaches 100%. Lets talk about our blob of 80 degree air. If we start cooling our blob of 80 degree air...it will eventually reach a temperature at which it can no longer hold the water vapor in it. Lets say that in this case our blob of air forms a cloud when we cool it to 50 degrees. Then 50 degrees in the dew point of our blob of air! Note that the dew point does not depend on the temperature like Relative Humidity! HOW YOU CAN MEASURE THE DEW POINT List of Materials: Do the following: Outside on a warm day in the spring or summer. If the dew point is below 32 degrees you will have to use antifreeze or another liquid that has been chilled to near zero in a freezer. Be careful! Antifreeze is poisonous..only use it with an adult nearby. Put a couple of inches of warm (80 F) water in a cup. Measure the temperature of the water, now add a few droppers of cold water and stir while measuring the temperature of the water. Keep adding a small amount of cold water to the metal cup until you see a thin film of water (water vapor)form on the outside of the cup. Make a note of the temperature of the water as soon as you see the water vapor form on the outside of the cup. The temperature you measured is the DEW POINT! As you added colder water to the warm water in the cup, the temperature of the water in the cup kept dropping. The sides of the cup got colder as the water inside got colder. The cup also chilled the air right next to it! When the temperature of the metal cup reached the dew point... The air just next to the cup was cooled by the dew point as well and water began to fall out of the air and stick to the cup! If you live in the western USA or at a high elevation, the dew point may be less than 32 degrees F, and you will see no condensation unless you put a very cold liquid, like salt water or antifreeze that is chilled to near zero in the cup. Title: How to Fix a Car That Shakes at Idle But Smoothes Out When Driving Introduction: A car that shakes or vibrates while idling can be quite unsettling and may indicate an issue with one or more of its components. However, if the shaking ceases once you pick up speed, it's likely that the problem lies within the engine idle control system. This tutorial will guide you through several possible solutions, starting from the simplest and least expensive to the most complex and costly. Please note that this guide is intended for informational purposes only; working on your vehicle always carries some risk, so consider consulting a professional mechanic before attempting any repairs. I. Check Engine Light and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) The forces between the atoms in a solid can take many forms. For example, a crystal of sodium chloride (common salt) is made up of ionic sodium and chlorine, which are held together by ionic bonds. In diamond or silicon, the atoms share electrons and make covalent bonds. In metals, electrons are shared in metallic bonding. Some solids, like most organic compounds, are held together with "van der Waals forces" coming from the polarization of the electronic charge cloud on each molecule. The dissimilarities between the types of solid come from the differences between their bonding. Title: How to Get to Albany International Airport from the Adirondack Northway Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of getting to Albany International Airport (ALB) from the Adirondack Northway (I-87), which is also known as the New York State Thruway. The airport serves the Capital District of New York state, including the cities of Albany, Schenectady, Troy, and Saratoga Springs. This tutorial assumes that you are starting your journey from the southbound lanes of I-87 but can be adapted for northbound travel with minor modifications. Key tips include familiarizing yourself with local signage, using a GPS or map application, and allowing extra time during peak travel periods. Step 1: Merge onto I-87 South (Adirondack Northway) To start your journey, merge onto I-87 South from your current location. If you're coming from locations such as Plattsburgh, Lake George, or Montreal, Canada, you would typically use this route. Keep right after merging to stay on I-87 S toward Exit 24 for ALB. Step 2: Follow signs for Exit 2W (Albany - Western Ave / US-20 W) As you approach Exit 2W, keep an eye out for signs indicating the direction for ALB. Take Exit 2W on the left side of the highway. This exit directs you towards both Western Avenue (US-20 West) and Albany International Airport. Key tip: During heavy traffic times, consider taking alternate routes suggested by your navigation system or map app to avoid potential congestion near the interchange. Step 3: Stay left at the fork, following signs for Albany International Airport After exiting I-87 South, proceed to the fork and stay in the left lane. Signs above should indicate 'Airport.' You want to follow these signs because they lead directly to the terminal area via NY-5 West (New York State Route 5). Step 4: Proceed westward along NY-5 West (towards Watervliet) Once you have merged onto NY-5 West, continue straight ahead. At this point, you'll pass under a pedestrian bridge connecting Wolf Road businesses with the passenger terminals. After about one mile, prepare to turn right into the airport entrance. Key guideline: Maintain a consistent speed while driving along NY-5 West; sudden braking might cause accidents due to high volumes of traffic entering and exiting nearby businesses and residential areas. Step 5: Turn right into the main entrance of Albany International Airport The main entrance to the airport will be clearly marked on your right-hand side. It has two lanes: one dedicated to taxis and buses, and another for personal vehicles. Enter the appropriate lane based on your mode of transportation. Step 6: Follow signs for Departures or Short Term Parking if dropping off passengers If you're dropping off passengers at the airport, look for signs leading you to either Departures or Short Term Parking upon entry. These signs will guide you to the designated drop-off zones located outside the ticketing level entrances. In conclusion, reaching Albany International Airport from the Adirondack Northway involves several steps requiring attention to road signage and adherence to traffic rules. April 8, 2011 B. 20 minutes C. Ms. Caldas D. Grade 1: Spelling II. BIG IDEA The students will understand that effective vocabulary builds social and academic knowledge. III. ESSENTIAL QUESTIONS 1. How will the students understand that effective vocabulary builds social and academic knowledge through spelling? IV. PENNSYLVANIA STATE STANDARDS 1.6.1.A: Listen actively and respond to others in small and large group situations with appropriate questions and ideas. V. GENERAL OBJECTIVES The students will understand how effective vocabulary builds social and academic knowledge. The students will understand how to spell different words VI. BEHAVIORAL OBJECTIVES The students will learn how to spell words by making words out of paper letter cubes provided for them. VII. INSTRUCTIONAL MATERIALS A. Blue pocket holder B. Note cards with different words on them C. Strip of paper letters IX. INSTRUCTIONAL PROCEDURES The teacher will hand out the strip of letters and tell the students that they will be spelling several words using these letters. The teacher will tell the students that they are playing a game. The teacher will tell them that they are going to try to make as many words as possible with the letters that were provided for them. The teacher will introduce the "Making Words" activity. The teacher will then hand out the letter strips and instruct the students to cut out each letter. The teacher will then instruct the students to place the words in a line, vowels first and then the consonants. The teacher will then say the first word aloud. The students will then spell the word with their letters. Once the students have spelled this word correctly, the teacher will call on a student to come up and use the note cards to spell out the world on the pocket holders. The teacher and students will repeat the process until all the words using the specific letters are spelled out correctly. At the end of the lesson, the teacher will give the students the opportunity to spell on big word using all the letters they have. This is called the "challenge" word. When a student(s) spells the challenge word correctly, one student will come up and spell out this word. D. STRATEGIES FOR DIVERSE LEARNERS This activity is great for all types of learners. The students are able to spell out the words using manipulatives which caters to hands on learners. Almost every student also gets the chance to come up to the pocket holder and spell out the word in front of the class with the note cards. This gets the students moving around and lets them get out of their seat. Once the students have spelled out the word correctly themselves, they also are able to see it visually in the front of the room. E. SUMMARY AND CLOSURE The students will try to figure out the challenge word. The teacher will then conclude with re-reading all the words that were created. The teacher will walk around the room to observe whether or not the students are spelling the words correctly. She will also see if the students are spelling the words correctly when they come up to the front of the room to spell the words for the class. XI. SUGGESTED INSTRUCTIONAL STRATEGIES W: The students are informed that they will be using their letter tiles to spell out words at their seats. They understand that the teacher will be walking around the room looking at everyone's spelling. One student will be asked to come up to the front of the room and spell the word for the class. H: To hold the students' interest the teacher will move through the game at a quick pace so the students are forced to race against time to spell the words quickly and correctly. E: The challenge word that is used to create the other words within it is a word that the students will hear in everyday life. All students, no matter what academic level, will be able to understand the definition of each word. R: The students will reflect and revisit each word in their daily writing during school. They will revise and rethink their words during the lesson especially if they spell the word incorrectly and have to go back to change it. E: The students will be able to express their understanding by spelling the words correctly at their desks with the word tiles they are given. Once the word is spelled correctly at the front of the room, the students will be able to self assess themselves and determine whether or not they had the correct spelling. T: The game is fast pace so when the higher level students have already spelled out the words and one of them is up in the front of the room spelling the word for the class, I will walk around to all of the students that are struggling and help them out as much as possible. These students are receiving help not only from their peers but from their teacher as well. O: When the words are first presented the teacher guiding the students. As soon as the word is said aloud the students are independently spelling. It [yellow wand] just will't pick up.
