HYDROSTATIC AND HYDRAULIC MACHINES CHAPTER LEARNING OBJECTIVES Upon completion of this chapter, you will be able to do the following: In this chapter we will discuss briefly the pressure of liquids: (1) hydrostatic (liquids at rest) and (2) hydraulic (liquids in motion). We will discuss the operation of hydrostatic and hydraulic machines and give applications for both types. You know that liquids exert pressure. The pressure exerted by seawater, or by any liquid at rest, is known as hydrostatic pressure. If you are billeted on a submarine, you are more conscious of the hydrostatic pressure of seawater. When submerged, your submarine is squeezed from all sides by this pressure. A deep-sea diving submarine must be able to withstand the terrific force of water at great depths. Therefore, the air pressure within it must be equal to the hydrostatic pressure surrounding it. PRINCIPLES OF HYDROSTATIC PRESSURE In chapter 9 you found out that all fluids exert pressure in all directions. That s simple enough. How great is the pressure? Try a little experiment. Place a pile of blocks in front of you on the table. Stick the tip of your finger under the first block from the top. Not much pressure on your finger, is there? Stick it between the third and fourth blocks. The pressure on your finger has increased. Now slide your finger under the bottom block in the pile. There you will find the pressure is greatest. The pressure increases as you go lower in the pile. You might say that pressure increases with depth. The same is true in liquids. The deeper you go, the greater the pressure becomes. However, depth isn t the whole story. Suppose the blocks in the preceding paragraph were made of lead. The pressure at any level in the pile would be considerably greater. Or suppose they were blocks of balsa wood-then the pressure at each level wouldn t be as great. Pressure, then, depends not only on the depth, but also on the weight of the material. Since you are dealing with pressure force per unit of area, you will also be dealing with weight per unit of volume-or density. When you talk about the density of a substance, you are talking about its weight per cubic foot or per cubic inch. For example, the density of water is 62.5 pounds per cubic foot; the density of lead is 710 pounds per cubic foot. However, to say that lead is heavier than water isn t a true statement. For instance, a 22-caliber bullet is the same density as a pail of water, but the pail of water is much heavier. It is true, however, that a cubic foot of lead is much heavier than a cubic foot of water. Pressure depends on two principles-depth and density. You can easily find the pressure at any depth in any liquid by using the following formula: P= Hx D P =pressure, in lb per sq in. or lb per sq ft H =depth of the point, measured in feet or inches D =density in lb per cu in. or lb per cu ft Note:If you use inches in your computation, you must use them throughout; if you use feet, you must use them throughout. What is the pressure on 1 square foot of the surface of a submarine if the submarine is 200 feet below the surface? Using the formula: P= Hx D P=200 x 62.5 = 12,500 lb per sq ft Every square foot of the sub s surface that is at that depth has a force of more than 6 tons pushing in on it. If the height of the hull is 20 feet and the area in question is between the sub s top and bottom, you can see that the pressure on the hull will be at least (200 10) x 62.5 = 11,875 pounds per square foot. The greatest pressure will be (200 + 10) x 62.5= 13,125 pounds per square foot. Obviously, the hull has to be very strong to withstand such pressures. Have you ever been in an airplane? Has he ever seen you drinking old water? = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PC-hm/leo33.cha = = = As a river flows towards its mouth, the countryside around the river often changes from hilly to flat. As it flows over the flat land the river becomes wider and slower. A wide slow river is called an 'old river'. An old river often floods across the land after there is lots of rain at the headwaters. An old river slowly builds up its banks on either side; the high banks are called levees. An old river often meanders (twists and turns), and sometimes, after a flood, it leaves lakes behind which are called ox-bows or billabongs. Old rivers are the most useful type of river for growing crops. Corn, rice, fruit, cotton, hay, tobacco and sugar are some of the crops that are grown near old rivers. Becoming heartless may seem like a daunting task, but with careful planning and dedication, it is possible to train yourself to feel less emotion and become more detached from situations and people. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to be heartless: Step 1: Understand What It Means to Be Heartless Before you begin your journey to becoming heartless, it's important to understand what this actually means. To be heartless is to lack compassion, empathy, and emotional connection towards others. This does not mean that you should strive to be cruel or malicious; rather, you are working towards building up mental walls that prevent strong emotions from affecting you negatively. Key Tip: Keep in mind that being heartless can have negative consequences if taken too far. It's essential to maintain a balance between feeling disconnected and retaining some level of emotional intelligence. Step 2: Analyze Your Emotions Start by paying close attention to your feelings throughout the day. Identify which situations trigger intense emotional responses and take note of them. By understanding these triggers, you can start working on desensitizing yourself to them over time. Guideline: Try keeping a journal where you record your daily experiences along with any significant emotional reactions. Reviewing this log regularly will help you track your progress and adjust your approach accordingly. Step 3: Practice Detachment " One quiet old lady whispering hush. The type of job she wanted right on the truck. |Photo Credit: Gabriel Pollard - swing / teeter totter| 1. Balance - Have a bigger kid sit on one end and a smaller on the other. The obvious will happen of course! The side holding more weight will bring the lighter side up. Now, have the larger kid scoot towards the center of the teeter totter away from the seat. What happened? By changing the placement of weight on the lever, the teeter totter will begin to balance the two weights. Have two kids sit on one side and one on the opposite. Using your science skills you gained from the above experiment, try to balance out the teeter totter to make it equal. 1. Force and Frequency - Give a student one small push on a swing and tell them not to pump. Count how many times the swing moves back and forth within one minute. Next, give a student one gigantic push (no pumping) and record the same data. Then, have a student pump their legs with no push and count. Compare your findings. Does a small or large push without pumping produce better results than using your legs to pump? Which way produced the largest number for the amount of times the swing went back and forth in one minute? via ehow 2. Twist a swing and let it fly. Time how long the swing takes to untwist. Then, have a kid sit on a a twisted swing. Have the child extend their arms and legs outward when you let go. You'll notice that the swing's speed will slow down. By pulling weight away from your body, you're slowing down the swing. Next, try tucking all the weight inward during a spin. Did you go much faster? 3. Body Position - Try sitting, standing, and squatting on a swing during a swinging cycle. How does this affect amplitude? Visit Odec to find out! Maybe it fits on the top of one of the containers. Sent in by: Anastasia of TX It's a tiny environment! - 2 liter soda bottle, cut in half - gallon-size resealable storage bag - potting soil - seeds - grass, beans, or whatever you have available - Check with a grown-up before you begin this. - A biome is an ecological community, like a rainforest, desert, or prairie. - Here's a way that you can experiment with how plants grow in different environments. It's a biome in a baggie. Here's how to make one. - First, pour pebbles into the bottom half of the soda bottle. The pebbles should be about a half an inch deep. - Then, pour some potting soil over the pebbles. Your biome should have about twice as much soil as pebbles. - Now, to plant the seeds. Make a trench down the center of the soil that's as deep as your fingernails. - Then sprinkle a pinch of seeds in the trench. - Cover it up with the soil. - Water the soil just until you see the water collect at the bottom of the pebbles. - Put the biome in a plastic bag and seal it. - Now, you've created an environment for your plants. You won't need to water your seeds again because the water will recycle itself. The roots of the plant absorb the water and the water travels up the stem to all the parts of the plant. When the water gets to the leaves, some of it evaporates. Some water also evaporates from the soil. The evaporated water forms drops on the bag. This is called condensation. The condensation then falls back down to the ground, like rain. This is called precipitation. This is the water cycle-evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. - Put your biome in a sunny place and in about three to four days your plants should start growing. The cool thing about a biome in a baggie is that everything your plants need is there. It's got water, nutrients from the soil, air from the bag, and it makes food from the sun. Now, here's how you can experiment with your biome in a baggie. Make a few different biomes and change the amount of light and water they get. This way you can see how your plants grow in different environments. A rainforest is a hot, wet climate but doesn't have a lot of light. A desert is hot and dry and doesn't have much water. A prairie has medium amounts of light and water. Eventually your plants are going to run out of carbon dioxide. Do some research and find out what you would need to keep your biome in a baggie going for a long time. Visit your local library or ask your parent or teacher for help. Then, send your discoveries to ZOOM. Sunshine, age 12 of Miramar Beach wrote: I planted mine on 11/6/10 so I hope it will grow by the time my science fair project is due. [to MOT about going farther back in the book] Creating a Rage Comic A rage comic is a type of webcomic that typically features simple stick figures and expressions known as "rage faces." These comics often depict humorous or relatable situations and can be created using various online tools or by drawing them yourself. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to create your own rage comic: ### Step 1: Choose a topic The first step in creating a rage comic is deciding what you want to depict. This could be something that happened to you recently, a common frustration, or just a silly joke. Having a clear idea of what you want to convey will help guide the rest of the process. Key tip: Try to choose a topic that will resonate with others and make them laugh or nod in agreement. ### Step 2: Plan out the sequence of events Rage comics usually consist of multiple panels, each showing a different part of the story. Before you start drawing or using an online tool, plan out the sequence of events so you know how many panels you need and what should happen in each one. Guideline: Aim for at least three panels but no more than eight. Too few panels may not provide enough context, while too many can make the comic feel drawn out. ### Step 3: Select your characters (rage faces) There are numerous pre-made rage faces available online, representing various emotions and reactions. Some popular ones include the classic "Angry Face," "Forever Alone," and "Me Gusta" (I like it). You can find these by searching for "rage faces" or "meme generators." Key tip: Use only a few distinct facial expressions throughout your comic to keep it from becoming confusing. ### Step 4: Draw or create your comic digitally If you prefer traditional methods, grab some paper and pencils and begin sketching out your comic based on the plans you made earlier. If you'd rather work digitally, there are several free tools available online such as Pixton, Strip Generator, or Make Beliefs Comix. These platforms allow you to customize your characters, backgrounds, and text bubbles easily. Guideline: Keep your drawings simple and focus on conveying emotion through exaggerated facial expressions and body language. ### Step 5: Add dialogue and sound effects Once you have your visual elements in place, add speech bubbles and any necessary sound effects to enhance the humor or impact of the scene. Be mindful of where you position these elements, ensuring they don't cover up important parts of the artwork and are easy to read. Key tip: Use concise phrases in your speech bubbles - short sentences are easier to understand quickly and maintain the flow of the comic. ### Step 6: Review and revise Before sharing your finished product, take some time to review it critically. Check for spelling errors, unclear sequences, or awkward layouts. Don't be afraid to make revisions if needed; sometimes small changes can significantly improve the overall quality of your comic. Guideline: Ask friends or family members for their opinions before publishing your work. Fresh eyes might spot issues you missed. ### Step 7: Share your creation Now that you've completed your rage comic, share it with others! There are plenty of websites dedicated to hosting user-generated content, such as Reddit, Imgur, or Tumblr. Alternatively, consider sharing it directly on social media platforms like Facebook or Twitter. Key tip: Remember to respect copyright laws when sharing your work - avoid using copyrighted material without permission. [clicks tongue] come on Mark do something like that. Lesson 3 Section 2 HOW TO NAME OR READ A DECIMAL Ignore the decimal point and read 038 as the whole number "Thirty-eight." The last digit, 8, falls in the thousandths place. When we read .038 as "Point 0, 3, 8," that is "spelling" the number, which is often convenient. But its name is "Thirty-eight thousandths." Ignore the decimal point, and read 002135 as the whole number 2,135 ("Two thousand one hundred thirty-five" Lesson 2, Question 4). The last digit 5 is in the millionths place. This is called a mixed number. The decimal point separates the whole number 14 on the left, from the decimal fraction on the right. In a mixed number, we read the decimal point as "and." Example 4. Write these in numerals: a) Two hundred four thousand b) Two hundred four thousandths c) Two hundred and four thousandths Example 5. Write in words: $607.08 Answer. Six hundred seven dollars and eight cents. Save "and" for the decimal point. Note that cents means hundredths. (Centum in Latin means 100.) 1 cent is the hundredth part of one dollar. We write 1 cent either as $.01 or 1 . When we write the cent sign , we do not write a decimal point. Example 6. Write "eighty cents" using the dollar sign $ and using the cent sign . Answer. $.80 80 At this point, please "turn" the page and do some Problems. Continue on to the next Section. Please make a donation to keep TheMathPage online. The lamp of life seemed to be spending itself to chase the vapours of a dungeon which no art could dissipate.-And to what purpose did she rally all her energy?-Was not the world a vast prison, and women born slaves? Though she failed immediately to rouse a lively sense of injustice in the mind of her guard, because it had been sophisticated into misanthropy, she touched her heart. Jemima (she had only a claim to a Christian name, which had not procured her any Christian privileges) could patiently hear of Maria's confinement on false pretences; she had felt the crushing hand of power, hardened by the exercise of injustice, and ceased to wonder at the perversions of the understanding, which systematize oppression; but, when told that her child, only four months old, had been torn from her, even while she was discharging the tenderest maternal office, the woman awoke in a bosom long estranged from feminine emotions, and Jemima determined to alleviate all in her power, without hazarding the loss of her place, the sufferings of a wretched mother, apparently injured, and certainly unhappy. A sense of right seems to result from the simplest act of reason, and to preside over the faculties of the mind, like the master-sense of feeling, to rectify the rest; but (for the comparison may be carried still farther) how often is the exquisite sensibility of both weakened or destroyed by the vulgar occupations, and ignoble pleasures of life? You are looking at the galactic plane. Imagine a flying saucer. It looks like a disk with a bulge in the middle. This is basically the shape of the Milky Way galaxy. The galactic plane is the part of the Milky Way which is in the "saucer" part of the shape. Much of what you see in the night sky is on the galactic plane of the Milky Way. This is because these stars are closer to us than stars in other galaxies; therefore, they seem brighter. Title: How to Be Civil When Talking About Politics Politics can be a heated and divisive topic, but it's important to remember that civil discourse is crucial for healthy conversations and understanding different perspectives. This tutorial will provide you with steps and tips on how to be civil when talking about politics. ### Step 1: Approach Political Conversations with an Open Mind Before engaging in political discussions, make sure you are open-minded and willing to listen to others' viewpoints. Entering a conversation with a rigid mindset may lead to arguments rather than productive dialogue. Being open-minded allows you to learn from others and consider alternative ideas, fostering respect and understanding between parties. Key tip: Before discussing politics, remind yourself that your goal is not to change someone's opinion but to understand their perspective better. ### Step 2: Understand Your Own Biases Everyone has biases based on personal experiences, values, and beliefs. Recognizing these biases beforehand enables you to engage more thoughtfully during political conversations. By being aware of your own preconceived notions, you become more receptive to other people's opinions and less likely to react defensively or emotionally. Key guideline: Take time for self-reflection and identify any potential biases you might have regarding specific issues or candidates. ### Step 3: Choose the Right Time and Place Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to draw and write in her journal. One day, Lily's mom asked her to clean her room. Lily said, "But mom, my room is already tidy!" Her mom replied, "I know, but can you please step on the toys and put them away?" Lily giggled and said, "Okay, mom!" She stepped on the toys and put them away in their proper place. After she was done, Lily went back to her journal and drew a picture of her clean and tidy room. She showed her mom and said, "Look, mom! My room is so pretty now!" Her mom smiled and said, "Good job, Lily! Your room looks great!" King was rushed to St. Joseph's Hospital. His heart had stopped. This lesson introduces the concepts of banking to children. - Identify a bank is one place where people save money. - Discuss and use the concept of interest. Banks are INTEREST-ing. What does that mean? Well, we use banks to save money and earn INTEREST, or borrow money and pay INTEREST. INTEREST-ing, isn't it? - Kid's Bank: A index of quizzes that go along with the stories on this website. - The Adventures of Dollar Bill: A story that teaches students about saving money in a bank. - The Adventures of Interest Ray: A story that teaches students about interest. - Dollar Bill Quiz: Assess students understanding of the story with this quiz. - Interest Ray Quiz: Assess students understanding of the story with this quiz. Start off the lesson with a series of questions that will focus the students and get them interested in learning more about interest. Discuss the various answers with the class. - Do you save money? - Where do you keep your savings? - Is it a good place to keep savings, or do you spend it? - Do you know that you could be earning money with your savings? - In what ways can you earn money with your savings? Now have the students read the story below to gain an understanding of savings and interest. A bank is a safe place to keep money. But it is so much more. Ask the students if they know what their money does when it is put into the bank. It doesn't just sit there. Instead, your money is used by the bank. One way that a bank uses your money is by giving people loans. Use the paragraphs below to explain to the students that they can get paid INTEREST if they put money in the bank. Also use the paragraphs below to explain that they have to pay INTEREST if they borrow money from the bank. INTERESTing! A bank lends money to people so that they can buy big purchases, like a car or a house. Then the people have to pay back the loan, plus a little more. That extra money they pay back is to say thanks to the bank for lending them the money. That money is called INTEREST. But hey, wait! Didn't the bank lend those people YOUR money? Why does the bank get paid back interest when it was your money? When you keep your money at the bank in a savings account, the bank also pays you! It is the bankers' way of paying you for letting them use your money. Can you guess what the money is called? If you said INTEREST you were right. The students will have learned about a savings account , a passbook, a deposit, a withdrawal, and a balance. They were also introduced to the concept of interest. Hello young learners! Today, let's talk about something fun and interesting - permutations! Have you ever played with a set of letters or numbers and tried to arrange them in different ways? That's basically what permutations are all about. Let's say you have a set of three letters: A, B, and C. You could arrange them in six different ways: ABC, ACB, BAC, BCA, CAB, and CBA. These six arrangements are called "permutations" of the set {A, B, C}. Now, imagine you have a longer set of letters or numbers. How many different permutations can you make? Well, there's a cool formula you can use to figure it out: P(n, r) = n! / (n-r)!. Here, "n" is the total number of items in your set, "r" is the number of items you want to include in each arrangement, and "!" denotes factorial, which means multiplying all positive integers up to that number. So, for example, 5! would equal 5 x 4 x 3 x 2 x 1 = 120. But why do we care about permutations? Well, they come in handy in lots of real-world situations. For example, if you're trying to solve a puzzle where you need to arrange tiles or pieces in a certain way, knowing how many possible permutations there are can help you figure out the solution more quickly. Or, if you're playing a game like Scrabble or Boggle, understanding permutations can help you find more words or combinations of letters. So next time you're playing around with letters or numbers, try arranging them in different orders and see how many different permutations you can create. It's a fun and easy way to practice math and build your creativity skills at the same time! Studies showed that the interaction between an autistic person and animals can increase communication, reduce stress, fear, aggression and reduce the severity of the symptoms of autism. When threatened, the ostrich will either hide itself by lying flat against the ground, or will run away. If cornered, it can attack with a kick from its powerful legs. Common predators of nests and young ostriches include jackals, various birds of prey, mongoose and vultures. Animals that prey on ostriches of all ages include cheetahs, lions, leopards, african dogs, and spotted hyena. Ostriches can often outrun their predators in a pursuit and can even outpace cheetahs over long distances. However, they may sometimes fight fiercely against predators, especially when chicks are being defended. They have sometimes killed lions in such fights. Title: How to Check Your iPhone's Phone Network Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through checking your iPhone's phone network. This is useful when you encounter issues such as poor signal strength, dropped calls, or slow data speeds. By following these steps, you can ensure that your device is connected to the correct network and troubleshoot any potential problems. Step 1: Accessing Settings App The first step is to access the settings app on your iPhone. You can do this by locating the grey gear icon on your home screen or apps page and tapping on it. The settings app allows you to customize various aspects of your iPhone, including its network connections. Key Tip: If you cannot find the settings app, swipe down from the middle of your home screen to open Spotlight Search. Type 'Settings' into the search bar and tap on the settings app when it appears. Step 2: Selecting Cellular Options Once you have opened the Settings app, scroll down until you see the option labeled 'Cellular'. Tap on this to proceed. Here, you can manage cellular data usage, enable or disable LTE, and view information about your current carrier. Guideline: For users with dual SIM cards, there will be two separate sections - one for each line. Make sure to select the correct SIM card if applicable. Step 3: Viewing Carrier Information Under the Cellular section, select 'Cellular Data Options'. On this menu, choose 'Network Selection'. Under this submenu, you should see the name of your current mobile carrier displayed under 'Voice & Data Network'. Detailed Explanation: At this stage, you are viewing crucial details regarding which network your iPhone is currently utilizing for voice calls (including texts) and mobile data. It verifies whether your device has successfully registered itself onto your service provider's infrastructure. Step 4: Manually Selecting A Network If for some reason you need to manually select a different network (for example, while traveling abroad), toggle off 'Automatic' at the top of the Network Selection screen. Afterward, available networks will populate below. Simply tap on the preferred network. Important Note: Unless necessary, leaving 'Automatic' selected is recommended because it ensures your iPhone automatically connects to the best possible network based on availability and signal strength. Step 5: Verifying Signal Strength Now let us check the signal strength indicator located at the top left corner of your iPhone's display. This shows bars representing the reception quality; more bars indicate stronger signals. Good Practice: To get an accurate reading, place your iPhone near a window or outside where there might be better reception. Poor indoor conditions could affect the accuracy of the signal readings. Conclusion: Following these steps enables you to confirm your iPhone's connection to the right phone network and diagnose any potential connection issues. Remember always to keep your iPhone updated to enjoy optimal performance and stability. Happy networking! My cat is with my doggie in Denver. Downvoting on Reddit using an iPhone or iPad is a straightforward process that can be accomplished by following these steps: 1. **Open the Reddit App:** The first step is to open the Reddit app on your iPhone or iPad. If you don't have the app installed, you can download it from the App Store. Once you have opened the app, you will be taken to the home screen where you can see various posts from different subreddits. 2. **Navigate to the Post You Want to Downvote:** Scroll through the feed until you find a post that you want to downvote. It could be because you disagree with the content, or you think it doesn't contribute to the discussion. Tap on the post to open it. 3. **Locate the Downvote Button:** Once you have opened the post, look at the bottom of the screen. Just below the comments section, you will see two arrow buttons - one pointing upwards ( ) and the other pointing downwards ( ). The upward-pointing arrow represents an upvote, while the downward-pointing arrow represents a downvote. 4. **Tap on the Downvote Button:** To downvote the post, tap on the downward-pointing arrow button. When you do this, the color of the arrow will change to indicate that you have successfully downvoted the post. A downvote indicates that you found the post unhelpful or irrelevant. 5. **Understand the Impact of Your Downvote:** Downvotes are used to help filter out low-quality content or off-topic discussions from the community. However, they should be used judiciously as excessive downvoting can suppress legitimate opinions or contributions. 6. **View Your Downvote History:** If you wish to view your downvote history, go to your profile page within the Reddit app. Tap on 'Settings' in the top right corner, then select 'Account Settings'. Under 'Data & Privacy', choose 'Profile Data & Visibility'. Here, you can request a download of your data which includes your voting history. Please note that this feature may vary depending on the version of the app and Reddit's policies. Key Tips and Guidelines: - Remember, downvoting is not meant to punish users but rather to improve the quality of content on Reddit. Always consider whether your reason for downvoting aligns with this principle before taking action. - Avoid downvoting based on personal prejudices or biases. Instead, focus on whether the post contributes positively to the conversation or adheres to the rules of the specific subreddit. - Be aware that frequent or indiscriminate downvoting might lead to temporary or permanent restrictions on your account according to Reddit's site-wide rules. - If you feel strongly against a particular post, consider commenting why instead of just downvoting silently. This encourages healthy discourse and constructive criticism. - Keep in mind that sometimes people post things humorously or sarcastically, so make sure you understand the context before deciding to downvote. - Lastly, if you accidentally downvote something, you can undo it by tapping the downvote button again. But act quickly since Reddit automatically hides voted posts after some time. Because it's all stuff I like except Swiss cheese. I know you get a princess telephone. Title: Date in Style After a Mind-Splitting Divorce =============================================== Introduction ------------ Divorce can be an emotionally draining experience that leaves you feeling overwhelmed and unsure about your future. However, with time, healing, and self-discovery, it is possible to move forward and start dating again. This wikiHow will guide you through the process of getting back into the dating scene after a mind-splitting divorce, emphasizing style, confidence, and healthy habits. ### Part 1: Healing from Your Divorce #### Step 1: Allow Time for Grieving * **Explanation:** Give yourself adequate time to grieve the end of your marriage before jumping back into the dating world. It's essential to work through your emotions and heal properly so you don't bring unresolved issues or negative energy into new relationships. * **Key Tips:** + Reflect on what went wrong and learn from past mistakes without blaming yourself excessively. + Seek professional help if needed (therapist, counselor, support group). + Practice self-care by engaging in activities that make you feel good physically, mentally, and spiritually. #### Step 2: Rediscover Yourself * **Explanation:** Take this opportunity to reconnect with who you are as an individual, separate from your former spouse. By understanding your values, passions, strengths, and weaknesses, you'll become more confident and attractive to potential partners. * **Key Tips:** + Try out new hobbies, classes, or sports. Title: How to Conclude a Paragraph A well-written paragraph is essential for effective communication in both academic and professional settings. A crucial part of writing a clear and coherent paragraph is crafting an appropriate conclusion that leaves a lasting impression on your reader. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to conclude a paragraph effectively, including detailed steps, helpful tips, and best practices. Step 1: Understand the Purpose of a Paragraph's Conclusion The primary goal of concluding a paragraph is threefold: a) To summarize or restate the main points discussed within the paragraph without being repetitive. b) To reinforce the significance or relevance of these ideas. c) To create a smooth transition towards the next paragraph or section while maintaining overall flow and continuity. Key Tip: Keep your audience in mind when drafting conclusions. Make sure they can grasp the importance of your discussion and feel compelled to read further. Step 2: Identify the Main Points Before writing a conclusion, identify the most critical aspects addressed in the paragraph. These could include arguments presented, evidence provided, examples given, or any other relevant information. The number of main points may vary depending upon the length and complexity of the paragraph. However, aim to highlight no more than two or three major takeaways. Guideline: Use signposts (words or phrases like 'in summary,' 'to sum up,' etc.) to signal that you are moving into the concluding phase of the paragraph. Step 3: Restate the Main Points Reformulate the identified main points using different wording. Avoid copying and pasting sentences directly from the body of the paragraph. Instead, paraphrase them to ensure variety and improved readability. Example: Original text: "Reading books expands our vocabulary, enhances our empathy, and provides us with knowledge about various subjects." Restated: "Exploring literature has numerous benefits such as improving our command over language, fostering emotional intelligence, and deepening our understanding of diverse topics." Step 4: Emphasize Significance or Relevance After restating the main points, emphasize their importance by connecting them back to the broader context of your paper, essay, or report. Explain why these findings matter or what implications they have for future research, policy decisions, or practical applications. Key Tip: Use transitional words and phrases ('therefore,' 'thus,' 'consequently,' etc.) to establish logical connections between your conclusions and subsequent discussions. Step 5: Create a Smooth Transition Ensure that the final sentence(s) lead seamlessly into the following paragraph or section. You can do this by introducing new concepts briefly or previewing upcoming content related to the current topic. Example: Transition statement leading into a new paragraph discussing potential solutions based on earlier findings: "Given the challenges outlined above, several promising strategies emerge for addressing climate change at both local and global levels..." Guideline: Refrain from introducing entirely new ideas in the conclusion; save those for separate paragraphs or sections. Step 6: Review and Revise Finally, review your concluded paragraph carefully to check if it meets all necessary criteria - clarity, conciseness, coherence, and effectiveness. Make revisions accordingly until satisfied with the result. Key Tip: Consider seeking feedback from peers or mentors during revision stages to gain alternative perspectives and improve overall quality. By consistently applying these steps and incorporating the provided guidance, you'll be able to write powerful and impactful conclusions for every paragraph you pen down. Remember, practice makes perfect, so keep practicing! How Trees Grow Kindergarten Oral Language Resources Children will: Learn about the concept of trees growing. Access prior knowledge and build background about trees. Explore and apply the concept of how trees grow. Children will: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of trees growing. Orally use words that describe trees. Extend oral vocabulary by speaking about what helps trees grow. Use key concept words [grow, plant]. Explain Use the slideshow to review the key concept words. Explain that children are going to learn about trees growing: The steps in helping a tree grow. How to help a tree grow. Model After the host introduces the slideshow, point to the photo on screen. Ask children: What do you see in this picture? (a tree, leaves, a trunk, branches). Ask children: Is the tree tall or small? (tall). Say: Trees are just like people, they start very small and grow to be big. Today we are going to learn how trees grow. What do you think trees need to grow? (food, water, care, etc.) Guided Practice Guide children through the next three slides, showing them that trees start as a small seed and grow bigger. Always have the children discuss whether they need the same things to grow. Apply Play the games that follow. Have them discuss with their partner the different topics that appear during the Talk About It feature. After the first game, ask children to talk about how a tree grows. After the second game, have them point to the top, middle, and bottom of a desk. Close Ask children: What do you need to do to help a tree grow? Explain. [GAR gets up from her chair to get something that is on the table] Formatting an SD card in a GoPro Hero 5 is a straightforward process that can help ensure optimal performance and storage capacity for your device. This tutorial will guide you through the steps involved, providing detailed explanations and helpful tips along the way. **Step 1: Power On Your GoPro Hero 5** Turn on your camera by pressing the power button located on the side of the device. Wait until the GoPro logo appears on the screen before proceeding to the next step. _Key Tip:_ Make sure your battery level is sufficient to complete the formatting process. It's recommended to have at least 50% charge remaining. **Step 2: Insert the SD Card into the Camera** Locate the microSD card slot on the bottom edge of the GoPro Hero 5. Carefully insert the SD card with its contacts facing downwards into this slot. Once inserted correctly, press gently until it clicks into place. _Guideline:_ Always handle your SD cards carefully to avoid damaging them physically or contaminating their contact points. **Step 3: Navigate to the Settings Menu** Press the back button (located above the power button) once to display the main menu. Scroll right using the mode/power button (on the front of the camera) until you reach the 'Settings' option. Press the shutter button (the larger one on top of the camera) to select 'Settings'. **Step 4: Select 'Delete' Option** In the settings menu, scroll down until you see the 'DELETE' option. Click on it to proceed. **Step 5: Choose Between 'All/Format'"** You'll be presented with two choices - 'ALL' and 'FORMAT'. If you want to delete all files from your SD card but keep the folder structure intact, choose 'ALL'. However, if you wish to completely erase everything including folders, then select 'FORMAT'. For most users looking to clear space or prepare the SD card for use in their GoPro, choosing 'FORMAT' would be appropriate. **Step 6: Confirm Deletion** A confirmation message will appear asking if you really want to format the card. Select 'Yes' to continue. Please note that this action cannot be undone, so make sure you don't need any data currently stored on the SD card before proceeding. **Step 7: Wait for Formatting Process Completion** The formatting process may take several seconds depending on the size of the SD card. During this time, do not remove the SD card or turn off your GoPro Hero 5. When completed, a success message will appear. **Step 8: Exit Back to Main Screen** To return to the home screen, simply press the back button repeatedly until you get there. Now your SD card is ready for capturing new memories with your GoPro Hero 5! By following these eight simple steps, you've successfully formatted your SD card in your GoPro Hero 5. Remember always to backup important data before formatting and regularly check your SD card health to prevent unexpected issues during shooting. You and the kids had some in the backyard yesterday. Title: How to Attract a Rebel Girl Introduction: Rebellious girls, often referred to as "rebels," are unique individuals who value independence, authenticity, and nonconformity. They can be incredibly alluring due to their strong personalities, creativity, and free-spirited nature. However, attracting a rebel girl requires understanding her mindset, respecting her individuality, and appreciating her unconventional qualities. Creating a hyperlink to an internal section of a webpage can help users navigate your site more efficiently and improve their overall experience. This tutorial will guide you through the process of creating internal links using HTML (HyperText Markup Language), which is the standard language used to create web pages. Step 1: Identify the target section First, identify the specific section within the same web page that you want to link to. Make sure this section has a unique identifier, often defined by an id attribute in the HTML code. If the target section doesn't have one yet, add an id attribute to its parent element, like so: ```html <h2 id="unique-identifier">Target Section Title</h2> ``` The value assigned to the id attribute should be descriptive but short, written in lowercase with hyphens separating words. Avoid spaces or special characters other than hyphens. Key tip: Using meaningful identifiers makes maintaining your website easier over time. Step 2: Create the anchor tag Next, create an anchor tag <a> at the source location from where you want to create the link. The basic structure of the anchor tag includes an href attribute pointing towards the target section: ```html <a href="#unique-identifier">Link Text</a> ``` Replace 'Link Text' with appropriate text describing what they would find when clicking the link. Keep it concise and relevant to encourage user engagement. Guideline: Accessibility matters! Use clear and informative link texts rather than vague phrases such as "click here" or "read more." Step 3: Specify the fragment identifier In the anchor tag's href attribute, "#unique-identifier" denotes the fragment identifier, guiding browsers directly to the targeted section with the corresponding id on the same webpage. For instance, if our target section was "<h2 id='about'>About Us</h2>", then our link becomes: ```html <a href="#about">Learn about us</a> ``` When someone clicks on "Learn about us," they are taken straight to the "About Us" header. Step 4: Test your internal link Finally, test your newly created internal link across various devices and browsers to ensure smooth functionality. Verify whether the link directs users accurately to the intended section without causing any errors. By following these steps, you now know how to effectively create an internal hyperlink in HTML. Not only does this technique enhance user navigation, but also contributes positively to search engine optimization (SEO) since well-structured websites tend to rank higher in search results. Happy coding! " Front feet, back feet, feet feet feet. " Here let me have your napkin we'll turn it the other way. You'll be in bed I'll you when I take care of them on Thursday. The I-IV-V Chord Progression Songwriting is all about choosing the right chords to make your song sound just the way you want it. But you might be surprised to learn that choosing chords at random is not the best way to make a tune sound good. There are standard and not so standard chord progressions that you can use as you write a song and harmonize it. The single most common chord progression you can learn is the I, IV, V progression. You can hear this progression all over the place in songs of just about any style. So, what is the I, IV, V progression? In any key there are a series of different chords that you can create. We often refer to these chords by the note of the scale they are based on. For example, in the key of C, we have the notes C D E F G A and B. The chord that starts with C is called the root chord or the (Roman numeral) I chord since it's the first note of the scale. The B chord, is called the II chord since it's based on the second note of the scale, and so on. So, in the key of C, the I, IV, V progression refers to the chords C, F and G. Play through these chords in sequence on your guitar so you can get a sense of how it sounds. Why does this progression sound so good? Well, first of all, they're all major chords. If you played a B chord in the key of C, it would be a diminished chord (the D and F are natural in the key of C). In the same way, the II and VI chords (D and A in this example) are minor in the key of C. Secondly, the V chord in any key naturally resolves very well back to the root chord. Play a G chord and then a C chord. Can you hear how it just seems to sound "complete" as if the song or passage is done? Try that with the II and VII chords if you like. Not quite as musically satisfying, is it? You get an even more complete sound by playing the V chord as a 7th chord. Try replacing the G chord in our example with a G7 chord. So you get C, F, G7, C. You can translate this progression into any key keeping the numbered chords in mind. For example, in the key of G your I, IV, V progression would be G, C, D or D7 and back to G. Try playing around with these chords and experiment to see which progressions you like the best. You can go in the big pool after we finish. Problem 23: A gene is a discrete sequence of DNA nucleotides. Determine the sequence of DNA. Your sister is a molecular biology graduate student. She invited you to visit her lab. Before she could give you a tour, her supervisor called a quick lab meeting. You're now sitting at her desk waiting for her to come back. Your sister has been trying to sequence a gene, and the autoradiogram of the sequencing gel is on her desk. You're going to read the sequence. From bottom to top, starting at the A, what is the sequence of the first 20 nucleotides? CTGCTGATGTTGAATTAGAG (No, the sequence starts with A.) ACTGCTGATGTTGAATTAGA (That is correct.) ACGTACGTACGTACGTACGT (No, these are just the four nucleotides repeated.) GACTAGTGCTCCTGGCCGTG (No, this is not correct.) You read as much of the sequence as you can and write the sequence down in your sister's notebook. Assuming that the DNA strand you just read is the 5' to 3' strand, what is the complement DNA sequence? Start with the 3' end of the DNA complement. 3'ACTGCTGATGTTGAATTAGA 5' (No, this is the 5' to 3' DNA sequence.) 3'ACUGCUGAUGUUGAAUUAGA 5' (No, this is an mRNA sequence.) 3'UGACGACUACAACUUAAUCU 5' (No, this is an mRNA sequence.) 3'TGACGACTACAACTTAATCT 5' (That is correct.) Adenine bonds with thymine; guanine bonds with cytosine. This is the complement DNA sequence. RNA polymerase reads the 3' to 5' DNA sequence to make mRNA. What is the mRNA sequence made in this example? 5'ACTGCTGATGTTGAATTAGA 3' (No, this is 3' to 5' DNA sequence.) 5'ACUGCUGAUGUUGAAUUAGA 3' (That is correct.) 5'UGACGACUACAACUUAAUCU 3' (No, this is not the correct mRNA sequence.) 5'TGACGACTACAACTTAATCT 3' (No, this is 3' to 5' DNA sequence.) mRNA has uracils instead of thymine. Notice that the mRNA is the same as the 5' to 3' DNA sequence with uracils replacing thymines. Your sister probably uses a computer program to decode the mRNA, but it's a short sequence so you use a codon table. What will the first 20 nucleotides of this mRNA sequence code for? Use as the stop codon. T A D V E L (Yes, if the ribosomes start with ACU codon; how do you know it starts here?) L L M L N* (Yes, if the ribosomes start with CUG codon; how do you know it starts here?) C C I R (Yes, if the ribosomes start with UGC codon; how do you know it starts here?) All of the above. (That is correct.) None of the above. (No, theoretically this mRNA can code for something.) Since you don't know where the gene starts, you have to consider all three frames of the sequence. You realize that you don't know how your sister got this sequence, so you really have to consider all six frames. After all, RNA polymerase may be reading the other strand for the "gene." What is the other mRNA that could be made? 5'UUAACGCGUGCCUCUGGUCU 3' (That is correct.) 5'TTAACGCGTGCCTCTGGTCT 3' (No, this is DNA sequence.) 5'TGACGACTACAACTTAATCT 3' (No, this is the DNA sequence in the wrong orientation.) The "other" mRNA sequence reads like the complement DNA with uracils replacing thymines. You deduce the codons for the other mRNA sequence. Based on what you know, which, if any, of these frames should your sister investigate first as being part of a gene? Frames 1, 2, 3 (No, frames 2 and 3 have stop codons and probably don't code for a protein.) Frames 2, 4, 5 (No, frame 2 has a stop codon and probably doesn't code for a protein.) Frames 1, 3, 5 (No, frame 3 has stop codons and probably doesn't code for a protein.) Frames 1, 4, 6 (That is correct.) Frame 1 (Yes, frame 1 is a possibility, but not the only one.) Frame 5 (Yes, frame 5 is a possibility, but not the only one.0 None of the above. (No, there are frames that may code for protein.) Frames 2, 3 and 5 are less likely candidates as genes because of the stop codons in the protein sequence. Frames 1, 4 and 6 are the good candidates as possible genes. The theory of evolution says that all living things on Earth, including plants, animals and microbes, come from a common ancestor by slowly changing throughout the generations. Darwin suggested that the way living things changed over time is through "natural selection". This is the better survival and reproduction of those that best fit their environment. Fitting into the place where you live is called adapting. Those who fit best into the place where they live, the best adapted, have the best chance to survive and breed. Those who are less well-adapted tend not to survive. If they do not survive well enough to raise young, this means they do not pass on their genes. In this way, the species gradually changes. Such diseases must be treated quickly and properly otherwise they can infect people on large scale and pose a danger to the world. It is very important that timely vaccination and treatment is provided. The study of epidemics is part of epidemiology. Does it need fixed or is it okay? I get my bow and arrow and shoot you. Title: How to Find the Volume of a Square Pyramid A square pyramid is a polyhedron with a square base and four triangular faces that meet at a single point called the apex. To find the volume of a square pyramid, you'll need to know its height and the length of one side of the square base. This article will guide you through the process using clear explanations and useful tips along the way. ### Step 1: Understand the Formula The formula for calculating the volume (V) of a square pyramid is given by: V = (1/3) area_of_base height This formula comes from the fact that a square pyramid can be divided into three smaller pyramids of equal size, each having the same height as the original but with bases being one-third of the area of the original base. Thus, finding the volume involves multiplying the area of the base by the height and then dividing this product by three. ### Step 2: Calculate the Area of the Base Since the base of our square pyramid is a square, we first calculate its area. The formula for the area (A) of a square is: A = side^2 Where 'side' represents the length of any side of the square. For example, if the side length measures 5 units, you would compute: A = 5^2 = 25 square units So now you have calculated the area of your square base! ### Step 3: Measure the Height Next, measure the perpendicular distance from the center of the base (which lies halfway along an edge since it's a square) to the apex of the pyramid. This measurement gives us the height (h). Make sure that your measuring tool is perpendicular to the base so as not to introduce error into your calculation. Let's assume, for instance, that the height measures 8 units. ### Step 4: Plug Values Into the Formula Now it's time to put everything together! Remember the volume formula? It was V = (1/3) area_of_base height. We already computed the area of the base (25 square units) and measured the height (8 units), so now just plug those values into the formula: V = (1/3) 25 8 ### Step 5: Multiply Perform the multiplication operation: V = (1/3) 200 ### Step 6: Divide Finally, divide the result by three: V = 200 / 3 Which equals approximately 66.67 cubic units when rounded to two decimal places. And there you have it - you've successfully found the volume of a square pyramid! Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Always double-check your measurements to ensure accuracy. Even small errors can significantly impact the final result due to the squaring involved in calculating the area of the base. 2. Be careful with unit conversions. If your base side length and height are given in different units (e.g., centimeters vs inches), convert them to the same unit before performing calculations. 3. Practice makes perfect! Try working out various examples until you feel comfortable with the procedure. Over time, you'll be able to perform these calculations quickly and accurately. Is he taking him by the hand? The human or primate hand is different from the paws, claws, and talons or other animals. The hand has opposable thumbs which are specifically designed for grasping objects. Your hands provide for both gross motor skills (grasping a large object) and fine motor skills (picking up a small pebble). Each hand has five fingers or digits. These are the thumb, index or pointer finger, middle finger, ring finger, and little finger or pinky. Each hand has 27 bones including the wrist. There are 14 bones in the fingers: 2 in the thumb and 3 in each of the other four fingers. These bones are called phalanges. There are 5 bones in the palm or metacarpus. These bones are called metacarpals. The hand is attached to the forearm by a joint called the wrist or carpus. There are 8 bones in the wrist. These bones are called carpals. The hands provide excellent tactile feedback, allowing you to sense and manipulate your environment. The fingertips are very dense with nerve endings. This makes your fingertips extremely sensitive to temperature, pressure, vibration, texture, and moisture. The toy that you bought at the circus. And Mrs Bausch said you can't xxx bring 'em home. Or XXX was the baby that she thought? Hey kids! Today, we're going to learn about something called "domains" in mathematics. Don't worry, it's not as complicated as it sounds! Have you ever played with a set of numbered blocks, where you can arrange them however you want? In math, we call this kind of set a "domain." It's just a fancy word for a group of numbers that we can use in a calculation. Now, let's think about a function. A function is like a machine that takes an input (a number) and gives us an output (another number). For example, imagine a machine that doubles whatever number you put into it. So if you give it a 3, it will give you back a 6. That's a function! When we talk about the domain of a function, we mean the set of all possible inputs that our machine can handle. Some functions have restrictions on what kinds of inputs they can take - for example, a function that divides by a certain number won't work if we try to divide by zero. But other functions, like addition or subtraction, can take any number as an input. So when we see a function like f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 7, we know that we can use any real number as an input. This is because squaring a number, subtracting another number from it, and adding a third number are all things we can do with any real number. In mathematical notation, we write the domain of this function as "x ", which means "x is a real number". So no matter what real number we choose, we can always plug it into this function and get a valid output. Cool, huh? Just remember: the domain of a function is simply the set of all possible inputs that the function can accept. And in many cases, especially with basic operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, the domain includes all real numbers. let's tie it back up now. Arrange group into a large circle with one person in the middle. The leader will prompt with the phrase Have you ever ? The person in the middle will finish the phrase. Example: Have you ever had candy bars for breakfast? Each of the people in the circle that has done what the person in the middle has said (had candy bars for breakfast) will quickly exchange places with someone else that has also done it. Whoever is left in the middle will finish the phrase the next time. The game has no real end so you can play 2 or 20 times, it s up to you! This post was submitted by David Deubelbeiss. The Peacock presented a memorial to Juno, importing how hardly he thought he was used, in not having so good a voice as the Nightingale; how that bird was agreeable to every ear that heard it, while he was laughed at for his ugly, screaming noise, if he did but open his mouth. The goddess, concerned at the uneasiness of her favourite bird, answered him very kindly to this purpose:--"If the Nightingale is blest with a fine voice, you have the advantage in point of beauty and size." "Ah!" says he, "but what avails my silent, unmeaning beauty, when I am so far excelled in voice? " The goddess dismissed him, bidding him consider that the properties of every creature were appointed by the decree of Fate; to him beauty, to the Eagle strength, to the Nightingale a voice of melody, to the Parrot the faculty of speech, and to the Dove innocence; that each of these was contented with his own peculiar quality; and, unless he wished to be miserable, he must also learn to be equally satisfied. However, most can be minimized with proper preparation. Some large fish, such as tuna (especially bluefin), can contain high levels of mercury. Tuna can cause mercury poisoning when consumed in very large quantities over time. Parasite infection by raw fish is not common in the modern world (less than 40 cases per year in the US). Infections can generally be avoided by boiling, burning, preserving in salt or vinegar, or freezing to a certain temperature. Title: How to Get Rid of Black Hair Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of removing black hair from various surfaces using safe and effective methods. This could be applicable if you're dealing with unwanted body hair or simply trying to clean up after a haircut or shedding. Remember that everyone has different types of hair and skin, so what works best may vary from person to person. Always do a patch test before applying any new product to your skin to avoid allergic reactions. Step 1: Vacuuming Explanation: The first step is to vacuum the area thoroughly. This will remove loose hairs which are easy to pick up mechanically. Tips: Use attachments provided with your vacuum cleaner like crevice tools and brushes for better results. Regularly changing the bag or emptying the canister also ensures optimal suction power. Step 2: Taping Explanation: For smaller areas, taping can be an efficient method. Stick tape (like duct tape) onto the hairy surface, press down firmly, then quickly peel it off. Repeat this until no more hairs stick to the tape. Key Guidelines: Be gentle when pulling off the tape to minimize discomfort and potential damage to your skin. Also, make sure the surface is dry as moisture can reduce the adhesiveness of the tape. Step 3: Using Adhesive Rollers Explanation: Similar to taping, adhesive rollers have a continuous strip of sticking material that rolls over the surface, picking up hairs as it goes along. They're often less messy than liquids or creams and can cover larger areas quicker than tape. Practical Tips: Roll slowly and steadily against the direction of hair growth. After several passes, wipe the roller clean or replace it if necessary. Step 4: Applying Hair Removal Cream Explanation: Hair removal creams work by breaking down proteins in the hair shaft, causing them to dissolve and become removable without cutting the follicle. Apply the cream evenly following package directions, wait specified time, then scrape away softened hair with a spatula provided. Important Note: Some people might experience irritation or allergies. If redness, burning sensation, or rash appears, wash off the product immediately and seek medical advice if needed. Step 5: Plucking Individual Hairs Explanation: Especially useful for facial hair or stray strands, plucking individual hairs one-by-one can provide longer lasting results compared to other temporary solutions. However, due to pain involved and possibility of ingrown hairs, it should be used sparingly. Helpful Tip: Dip tweezers in rubbing alcohol before and after usage to sterilize them and decrease risk of infection. Step 6: Waxing Explication: Hot wax or cold wax strips can effectively pull out multiple hairs at once. While typically associated with beauty salons, there are home kits available too. Make sure to read all instructions carefully and perform a patch test prior to full application. Safety Measures: Never heat wax above recommended temperature to prevent burns. Additionally, don't reuse wax sticks to avoid cross contamination. Conclusion: Remember, consistency is key when getting rid of black hair. Depending on the quantity and location, some methods may prove more effective than others. It's always wise to consult professional opinions if unsure about certain techniques or products. An equation is a mathematical statement that has an expression on the left side of the equals sign (=) with the same value as the expression on the right side. An example of an equation is 20 + (- 60) = - 40. One of the terms in an equation may not be know and needs to be determined. Often this unknown term is represented by a letter such as x. (e.g. 20 + x = - 40). The solution of an equation is finding the value of the unknown x. To find the value of x we can use the subtraction property of equations which says: "The two sides of an equation remain equal if the same number is subtracted from each side." We may also use the addition property of equations which says: "The two sides of an equation remain equal if the same number is added to each side." - 50 + x = 40 - 50 + x + 50 = 40 + 50 0 + x = 90 x = 90 Check the answer by substituting the answer (90) back into the equation. - 50 + 90 = 40 Do you have gym do you height take your shoes off if you have gym or do you no... Title: How to Break a Headfirst Fall or Dive Introduction: Falling headfirst can be a scary experience, but knowing how to break your fall can help minimize the risk of injury. This tutorial will guide you through the steps to safely break a headfirst fall or dive. It's important to note that this information is meant to serve as a general guideline; individual results may vary based on various factors such as height, surface, and physical condition. Always prioritize safety and seek professional training when possible. Step 1: Stay Calm Maintaining composure during a potential fall is crucial. Panic can lead to poor decision-making and increased risk of injury. Tell yourself to stay calm and focus on executing the following steps. Step 2: Tuck Your Chin Tucking your chin protects your neck and spine by reducing the distance your head travels during the fall. To do this, simply bend your head downward so your chin touches your chest. Keep your mouth closed to prevent damage to your teeth and tongue. Step 3: Turn Your Body Sideways (If Possible) Turning your body sideways disperses the impact across a larger area, reducing pressure on any single point. Ideally, try to land on your shoulder and hip instead of directly on your back or stomach. However, if turning isn't feasible due to time constraints or other factors, proceed with tucking your chin and moving onto Step 4. Step 4: Bend Your Elbows and Knees Bending your elbows and knees absorbs some of the shock from the fall. As you descend, keep your arms loose at your sides, then bend them at the elbow upon impact. Similarly, slightly bend your knees just before hitting the ground. Avoid fully extending your limbs, which could result in fractures. Step 5: Roll Through the Fall (Optional) Rolling through the fall further distributes the force over a wider surface area, lessening the chance of serious harm. If conditions allow, attempt to roll once you make contact with the ground. Start by rolling onto your shoulder, followed by your back, and finally ending up on your opposite shoulder. Remember to maintain control throughout this process to avoid unintentionally twisting your body. Step 6: Protect Vulnerable Areas Certain areas of the body are more susceptible to injuries than others. For instance, wrists are prone to breaking when trying to brace oneself during a fall. Instead, aim to distribute the weight evenly between your forearms and palms while keeping them close together. Also, shield your face and head using your tucked chin and bent arms. Key Tips and Guidelines: * Practice proper falling techniques under supervision until they become second nature. * Never intentionally jump headfirst into a situation without assessing potential risks and ensuring appropriate safety measures are in place. * Regular exercise and strength training can improve overall fitness, making it easier to absorb impacts from falls. * Consult healthcare professionals for personalized advice regarding falling prevention and recovery strategies tailored to specific health concerns or physical limitations. Conclusion: While breaking a headfirst fall requires quick thinking and precise execution, understanding these steps can significantly reduce the likelihood of severe injuries. By practicing safe falling techniques and maintaining awareness of one's surroundings, individuals can mitigate risks associated with unexpected plunges or dives. Document: Diagram of seismic wave movement There are two types of waves that can be used to study the Earth's interior: - P-waves are Primary or Compressional Waves. Faster waves that have speeds of 5 km/s at the top of the crust, 8 km/s at the top of the mantle, and 14 km/s at the bottom of the mantle. They can travel through liquids and so can pass through Earth's liquid outer core. - S-waves are Shear or Transverse waves. Slower than P-waves, they cannot travel through liquids and do not pass through the Earth's core. - File type: - File size: - 25 kB - Australian Museum [Nina has run out of the room.] Didn't Ross and I get home very lately today? Title: How to Hit a Home Run in Wii Sports Wii Sports is a popular sports video game developed by Nintendo for its Wii console. The baseball segment of the game allows players to swing their remote as if they were holding an actual baseball bat, making it both fun and challenging. Hitting a home run can be quite difficult, but with practice and patience, you'll get there! Here's our step-by-step guide on how to hit a home run in Wii Sports. ### Step 1: Select Your Character and Difficulty Level Choose your Mii character carefully; different characters have varying abilities that might affect performance. For beginners, lower difficulty levels are recommended until you become more comfortable with the controls. As you progress, gradually increase the level of difficulty for a greater challenge. **Key Tip:** Characters with higher pitches per minute (PPM) rates tend to throw faster, which could make hitting harder. Consider this when selecting your opponent. ### Step 2: Positioning Your Character At the start of each turn, position your player at home plate using the directional pad on the Wiimote. Ideally, place them slightly towards the side where you feel most comfortable swinging, leaving enough room to cover both inside and outside pitches. **Guideline:** If you're right-handed, stand closer to the left edge of the box; if you're left-handed, vice versa. This gives you better reach for pitches over the center or opposite field. ### Step 3: Timing Is Everything Wait for the pitcher to release the ball before initiating your swing. Pay attention to the color of the approaching ball - green means easy, yellow medium, red hard. Adjust your timing accordingly; early swings work best for fastballs while late swings suit breaking balls. **Key Tip:** To perfect your timing, listen closely to the sound cues given during the windup and release of the ball. These sounds indicate when to begin your swing motion. ### Step 4: Swinging Technique Hold the Wiimote horizontally, mimicking the grip of a real baseball bat. When ready to swing, raise the controller upwards then swiftly bring it downward through the 'strike zone'. Make sure to fully extend your arm for maximum power behind your shot. **Guideline:** A good strategy is to aim high, especially against slower pitches. High hits often result in homers due to less defensive interference. ### Step 5: Follow Through After contact, continue moving the Wiimote forward as if following the trajectory of the batted ball. Proper follow-through ensures optimal force transfer from the Wiimote to the virtual ball, increasing your chances of sending it flying out of the park. **Key Tip:** Keep practicing your swing technique until it feels natural and smooth. Consistency leads to improved results. ### Step 6: Anticipate Curveballs Curveballs can disrupt even the most seasoned players' rhythm. They move differently than regular pitches, so anticipation becomes crucial here. Look for clues like a wider windup, jerky movements, or changes in the pitch speed indicator. **Guideline:** Practice recognizing these signs and adjust your swing timing to match the anticipated curveball path. Remember, mastering the art of hitting home runs takes time and persistence. Don't be discouraged by initial struggles; keep refining your skills and strategies, stay patient, and enjoy the process. Happy gaming! Put this on your hand and let them give a big hug. Some words by default refer to males, and the feminine counterparts have to be constructed by adding the "-in-" suffix to the masculine root. Such a words are words like ' (father) and ' (mother), ' (son) and ' (daughter), ' (uncle) and ' (aunt), and so on. The majority of all Esperanto words have no specific meaning on the basis of sex. Some people proposed the suffix "-i -" with male meaning in order to make the meaning of the basic word neutral. However this proposal is not widely accepted by Esperanto speakers. Finding the polynomial function zeros is not quite so straightforward when the polynomial is expanded and of a degree greater than two. One method is to use synthetic division, with which we can test possible polynomial function zeros found with the rational roots theorem. Once we find a zero we can partially factor the polynomial and then find the polynomial function zeros of a reduced polynomial. We're finding the zeros of polynomial functions. Let me show you two examples: f(x)= 2(x+3) and x 1(x+10). If you're given a polynomial like this, it's really easy to find the zeros of the function because each of these factors contributes a 0. So you'll have 3, 1, and 10. You're generally not going to get a problem this easy. What about g(x), which is not factored? This is the kind of problem you'll see: Find the zeros of g(x). Well there are generally three steps we have to think about. First, use the rational roots theorem to find potential zeros. These are potential zeros. They don't necessarily work but we have to check them. And we use synthetic division to test the potential zeros. And finally, we'll factor out whatever factor corresponds to the 0, and we'll get a reduced polynomial that will contain the rest of the zeros. Let's see how this works with the example, g(x). So I want to find all the zeros of this polynomial function. Now the rational roots theorem says to look at the integer factors of the leading coefficient and the constant. Now the leading coefficient is 1; its integer factors are 1 and 1. For example, 1x1 is 1, and 1x 1 is 1. So those are integer factors of 1. And for 10, we look at plus or minus1 or plus or minus 2, plus or minus 5, and plus or minus 10. We can take two of these numbers, multiply them, and get 10: 1 and 10 would work. Now the rational roots theorem says that the potential zeros are these integer factors divided by these. So it's plus or minus 1, plus or minus 2, plus or minus 5, and plus or minus 10, all over plus or minus 1. Of course, that's just going to give you the stuff on the numerator, this doesn't really contribute anything. So what I usually like to, and I suggest you do too, is start with the easy ones. Start with one and 1. So let's start with 1. Now we're going to need synthetic division to test these. The way synthetic division works is I'm using one as my 0, and I write the coefficients of this polynomial down here: 1, 1, 8, and 10. The one comes down, I multiply and write the result here. So 1x1 is 1. Then I add and I get 0. Then I multiply again, 1x0, and I write the result here. And I add again, 8, and I multiply, 1x8 is eight, and I get 18. This last number is my remainder. Now the remainder's not 0, so one is not a 0. That means that g(1) is actually 18. You remember the remainder theorem. This is actually the value of the function g(1). So one didn't work. Let's try 1. Same coefficients 1, 1, 8, and 10, and the same process. Bring the one down and multiply. Add and multiply. Add and multiply and that works. The remainder's 0 and that means that 1 is a 0, and that means that x 1 is a factor. And by the way, these are the coefficients of the other factor. So this function can be written g(x)= one factor is (x 1)(x+1). The other factor is x2 2x+10; this is the reduced polynomial. Now if you want to find the remaining zeros of this function, you've got to look here. This one has a 0 of 1. This has the other two zeros. Now it's a quadratic, so we can use the quadratic formula: a is 1, b is 2, and c is 10. So b, which is +2, b2; 2 squared is 4; 4ac, or 4x1x10 is 40, all over 2a. So this is going to give me 2 root negative 36. It's imaginary. Root negative 36 is 6i. So 2( 6i)/2 is 1( 3i). And that represents two zeros; two imaginary zeros for our polynomial. So the zeros are 1, 1+3i, and 1 3i. Now remember what we did. First, we used the rational roots theorem to find potential zeros. We had all these potential zeros. We were lucky to find one of them so quickly. But I would always check one and 1 first; the arithmetic is going to be the easiest. Then once you find a 0, you can take the reduced polynomial and looks for the zeros of that. Now with the reduced polynomial, if you start with a fourth degree, the reduced polynomial will be a cubic, and you have to do more of this synthetic division. But when you finally do get a quadratic, use the quadratic formula and find the remaining zeros that way. Baby is as big as you are! The orange-colored bands played "Auld Lang Syne," and a louder mazarine band came across them with "Oh, whistle and I will come to thee, my lad"--probably as the tune the most symbolical of Liberalism which their repertory would furnish. There was not a single club bearing the Radical blue: the Sproxton Club members wore the mazarine, and Mr. Chubb wore so much of it that he looked (at a sufficient distance) like a very large gentianella. It was generally understood that "these brave fellows," representing the fine institution of Benefit Clubs, holding aloft the motto, "Let brotherly love continue," were a civil force calculated to encourage voters of sound opinions and keep up their spirits. But a considerable number of unadorned heavy navvies, colliers, and stone-pit men, who used their freedom as British subjects to be present in Treby on this great occasion, looked like a possible uncivil force whose politics were dubious until it was clearly seen for whom they cheered and for whom they groaned. Thus the way up to the polling-booths was variously lined, and those who walked it, to whatever side they belonged, had the advantage of hearing from the opposite side what were the most marked defects or excesses in their personal appearance; for the Trebians of that day held, without being aware that they had Cicero's authority for it, that the bodily blemishes of an opponent were a legitimate ground for ridicule; but if the voter frustrated wit by being handsome, he was groaned at and satirized according to a formula, in which the adjective was Tory, Whig, or Radical, as the case might be, and the substantive a blank to be filled up after the taste of the speaker. Her niece had a quiet name for her--she kept it quiet; thinking of her, with a free fancy, as somehow typically insular, she talked to herself of Britannia of the Market Place--Britannia unmistakable, but with a pen in her ear, and felt she should not be happy till she might on some occasion add to the rest of the panoply a helmet, a shield, a trident and a ledger. It was not in truth, however, that the forces with which, as Kate felt, she would have to deal were those most suggested by an image simple and broad; she was learning, after all, each day, to know her companion, and what she had already most perceived was the mistake of trusting to easy analogies. There was a whole side of Britannia, the side of her florid philistinism, her plumes and her train, her fantastic furniture and heaving bosom, the false gods of her taste and false notes of her talk, the sole contemplation of which would be dangerously misleading. She was a complex and subtle Britannia, as passionate as she was practical, with a reticule for her prejudices as deep as that other pocket, the pocket full of coins stamped in her image, that the world best knew her by. She carried on, in short, behind her aggressive and defensive front, operations determined by her wisdom. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Gilliam/TD/7m/46746nj.cha = = = [holds up one of the little animal figures] This is a cute book, isn't it? When we went down t' the square. Your child will love to spell out the objects in this alphabet art project with pasta letters. This pasta craft opens a new art form for your child. What You'll Need: - Alphabet pasta - Craft glue - Drawing or construction paper This art form, known as word graphs, uses words to draw an outline of an object. For example, the words sun and shine form a sun's shape. The word sun is used to outline the round part of the sun, and the word shine is used to outline the sun's rays. Step 1: Gather letters from alphabet pasta so your child can make a picture with words. If you don't have any alphabet pasta, you can still make a word graph -- just draw the letters. Step 2: Glue the alphabet pasta to a piece of paper to make the picture. Fill in any missing letters or add more details with markers. Actually I don't play with these things [magnets] [laughs]. Connecting the positive of one cell to the positive of the other, and the negative to the negative is called connecting them "in parallel". The voltage stays the same, but the current is added together. Voltage is the pressure pushing the electrons through the wires, it is measured in volts. Current is how many electrons can go at once, it is measured in amps. The combination of current and voltage is the power (watts = volts x amps) of the battery.
